content.summary: Propositional logic provides the syntax and semantics of mathematical proof and digital circuits. Valid inference rules and logical equivalence underpin rigorous argument throughout mathematics and computer science.

framing.relevance: Truth-functional logic supports proof, algorithms, and circuit design.

content.title: Pythagorean Theorem
content.definition: In a right triangle with legs a and b and hypotenuse c, a² + b² = c², relating side lengths through the square of the longest side.
content.summary: The Pythagorean theorem is the central relationship of right-triangle geometry and underpins distance formulas in coordinate and vector settings. It appears in trigonometric identities, physics vector components, and error metrics.

framing.relevance: Right-triangle side relation extends to distance in coordinate and vector geometry.

content.title: Quadratic Equations
content.definition: Equations of the form ax² + bx + c = 0 (a ≠ 0), solved by factoring, completing the square, or the quadratic formula, with up to two real solutions.
content.summary: Quadratic equations model parabolic motion, area optimization, and numerous applied problems. Understanding their solution methods and the discriminant prepares students for conic sections, complex numbers, and calculus applications.

framing.relevance: Quadratics link algebraic solving to parabolic graphs and applied optimization.

content.title: Random Variables
content.definition: A random variable maps outcomes to numbers; discrete variables have probability mass functions, continuous variables have probability density functions with total probability 1.
content.summary: Random variables translate probabilistic events into numeric quantities amenable to expectation and variance. They are the language of statistical models from genetics to quality control.

framing.relevance: Random variables connect probability models to expected values and spread.

content.title: Rational Functions
content.definition: A rational function is a ratio of polynomial functions, f(x) = p(x)/q(x), with domain excluding zeros of the denominator and possible vertical asymptotes.
content.summary: Rational functions model inverse variation, rates with limiting behavior, and asymptotic growth. Analyzing holes, asymptotes, and domains prepares students for integration techniques and differential-equation models.

framing.relevance: Rational functions introduce asymptotic behavior central to limits and rates.

content.title: Ratios and Proportions
content.definition: A ratio compares two quantities; a proportion states that two ratios are equal, enabling scaling and solving for unknown values via cross-multiplication.
content.summary: Ratios express relative size without fixed units, while proportions formalize equality of rates. These tools appear in unit conversion, similar figures, and linear modeling throughout mathematics and the sciences.

framing.relevance: Core proportional reasoning used in algebra, geometry similarity, and data interpretation.

content.title: Second-Order Linear Differential Equations
content.definition: Equations of the form ay″ + by′ + cy = g(x) with constant coefficients are solved via characteristic equations and particular solutions for driving terms.
content.summary: Second-order linear ODEs model oscillators, circuits, and mechanical vibrations. The superposition principle and characteristic-polynomial method extend naturally to higher-order and system formulations.

framing.relevance: Second-order ODEs model oscillation and resonance in physical systems.

content.title: Separable Differential Equations
content.definition: A first-order ODE is separable if it can be written g(y) dy = h(x) dx, solved by integrating both sides after separating variables.
content.summary: Separation of variables is often the first exact technique students apply to ODEs and yields closed-form solutions for many growth and decay models. The method foreshadows integrating factors and transform techniques.

framing.relevance: Variable separation solves many introductory growth and decay models.

content.title: Set Theory
content.definition: A set is a collection of distinct objects; operations include union, intersection, complement, and Cartesian product, with Venn diagrams illustrating relationships.
content.summary: Set theory is the language of modern mathematics for defining functions, relations, and probability sample spaces. Mastery of set operations supports counting principles and formal proof.

framing.relevance: Sets formalize collections underlying functions, relations, and probability.

content.title: Systems of Linear Equations
content.definition: A collection of two or more linear equations in the same variables, solved simultaneously by substitution, elimination, or matrix methods to find values satisfying all equations.
content.summary: Systems model constraints that must hold together, such as intersecting lines or balancing chemical equations. Solution techniques foreshadow linear algebra and appear throughout multivariable calculus and applied modeling.

framing.relevance: Simultaneous linear constraints are foundational for linear algebra and modeling.

content.title: Systems of Differential Equations
content.definition: A system of ODEs couples several unknown functions, often written as x′ = Ax + f(t) for a matrix A, with solutions describing interacting dynamical variables.
content.summary: Coupled ODEs model populations, circuits, and mechanical linkages where variables co-evolve. Matrix exponential and eigenvector methods classify stability and decouple interacting modes.

framing.relevance: Coupled ODEs describe interacting dynamical systems in science and engineering.

content.title: Triangle Properties and Angle Relationships
content.definition: The sum of interior angles in a triangle is 180°, with classifications by side length (equilateral, isosceles, scalene) and angle measure (acute, right, obtuse).
content.summary: Triangle properties connect angle arithmetic to geometric proof and coordinate methods. They support similarity, trigonometry, and vector decomposition in later courses.

framing.relevance: Triangle facts anchor Euclidean geometry and trigonometric definitions.

content.title: Trigonometric Ratios
content.definition: In a right triangle, sine, cosine, and tangent are ratios of side lengths relative to an acute angle: sin θ = opposite/hypotenuse, cos θ = adjacent/hypotenuse, tan θ = opposite/adjacent.
content.summary: Trigonometric ratios quantify angle-size relationships in triangles and extend to all real angles via the unit circle. They are essential for modeling waves, rotations, and periodic behavior in physics and engineering.

framing.relevance: Right-triangle ratios generalize to circular functions used throughout calculus.

content.title: Unit Circle and Radian Measure
content.definition: Radians measure arc length per unit radius; on the unit circle (radius 1), coordinates (cos θ, sin θ) define trigonometric values for all real θ.
content.summary: Radian measure aligns angle size with arc length and is the standard unit in calculus. The unit circle extends trigonometry beyond right triangles and supports analysis of periodic functions and complex exponentials.

framing.relevance: Radians and the unit circle are prerequisite to calculus and complex analysis.

content.title: Vector Fields
content.definition: A vector field assigns a vector to each point in a domain, modeling flows, forces, and gradients of scalar potentials.
content.summary: Vector fields describe how quantities vary through space, from fluid flow to electromagnetic force. They connect partial derivatives to line and surface integrals via Green's, Stokes', and divergence theorems.

framing.relevance: Spatial vector assignments model flow, force, and conservative fields.

content.title: Vector Spaces and Subspaces
content.definition: A vector space is a set closed under addition and scalar multiplication satisfying axioms; a subspace is a subset that is itself a vector space, often described as span, null space, or column space.
content.summary: Vector spaces abstract the structure shared by geometric vectors, function spaces, and solution sets of homogeneous systems. Subspace concepts organize linear independence, basis, and dimension.

framing.relevance: Abstract vector spaces unify geometry, systems, and function spaces.

content.title: Vectors in Two and Three Dimensions
content.definition: A vector has magnitude and direction, represented by components or as a directed segment; operations include addition, scalar multiplication, dot product, and cross product in ℝ³.
content.summary: Vectors encode displacement, force, and velocity in multiple dimensions. Vector algebra supports lines and planes in space, multivariable differentiation, and physical field descriptions.

framing.title_in_context: Vectors in Two and Three Dimensions
framing.relevance: Vector methods extend calculus and geometry to higher dimensions.

hierarchy_location.topic: Vectors in Two and Three Dimensions

content.title: Abnormal Uterine Bleeding
content.definition: PALM-COEIN classification guiding structural versus hormonal evaluation and treatment.
content.summary: Fibroids and polyps are structural causes. Anovulatory bleeding common in PCOS.

framing.relevance: Core obstetrics & gynecology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Clinical Acid-Base Disorders
content.definition: Metabolic and respiratory acidosis-alkalosis diagnosed by pH, PaCO2, and bicarbonate with compensation.
content.summary: Anion gap metabolic acidosis includes MUDPILES causes. Winter formula estimates respiratory compensation.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acne and Rosacea
content.definition: Follicular inflammatory disorders managed with topical retinoids, antibiotics, and isotretinoin for severe acne.
content.summary: Isotretinoin requires pregnancy prevention. Rosacea triggers include alcohol and heat.

framing.relevance: Core dermatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute Abdomen Evaluation
content.definition: Surgical versus medical abdominal pain with imaging, labs, and operative indications.
content.summary: Peritonitis suggests surgical emergency. Free air on imaging indicates perforation.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute Coronary Syndrome
content.definition: Unstable angina, NSTEMI, and STEMI spectrum with plaque rupture and myocardial ischemia.
content.summary: STEMI requires reperfusion therapy. Troponin elevation distinguishes MI from unstable angina.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute Kidney Injury Management
content.definition: Prerenal, intrinsic, and postrenal AKI with fluid resuscitation, nephrotoxin avoidance, and renal replacement.
content.summary: FeNa helps distinguish prerenal from ATN. Indications for dialysis include refractory hyperkalemia.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute Pancreatitis
content.definition: Gallstone and alcohol etiologies with inflammatory cascade, pain control, and fluid resuscitation.
content.summary: Lipase elevation supports diagnosis. Severe pancreatitis risks necrosis and organ failure.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome
content.definition: Noncardiogenic pulmonary edema with severe hypoxemia requiring lung-protective ventilation.
content.summary: Berlin criteria define ARDS by timing, imaging, and oxygenation. Low tidal volume ventilation improves outcomes.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Adrenal Insufficiency
content.definition: Primary Addison versus secondary cortisol deficiency with mineralocorticoid loss in primary disease.
content.summary: Hyperpigmentation suggests primary insufficiency. Stress steroid dosing prevents adrenal crisis.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Anemia Diagnostic Approach
content.definition: Reticulocyte count, MCV, and peripheral smear guiding mechanistic workup of anemia.
content.summary: High reticulocytes imply hemolysis or bleeding. Low reticulocytes suggest production failure.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Anesthesia Basics
content.definition: General, regional, and local anesthesia with airway management and malignant hyperthermia awareness.
content.summary: Spinal anesthesia for lower abdominal surgery. Succinylcholine contraindicated in malignant hyperthermia susceptibility.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Regional and general anesthesia choices affect orthopaedic ERAS pathways, multimodal analgesia, and pain control after TJA.

content.title: Appendicitis
content.definition: Inflamed appendix presenting with periumbilical pain migrating to RLQ requiring appendectomy.
content.summary: Alvarado score supports diagnosis. Perforation increases morbidity especially in children.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Arrhythmia Mechanisms
content.definition: Reentry, enhanced automaticity, and triggered activity disrupting normal cardiac rhythm.
content.summary: Reentry requires unidirectional block and slow conduction. Long QT predisposes to torsades de pointes.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Asthma Diagnosis and Management
content.definition: Reversible bronchospasm with stepwise controller and reliever therapy including inhaled corticosteroids.
content.summary: Spirometry shows obstructive pattern with bronchodilator response. SABA relieves acute symptoms.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Atrial Fibrillation Management
content.definition: Rate versus rhythm control, anticoagulation by stroke risk, and cardioversion considerations.
content.summary: CHA2DS2-VASc guides anticoagulation. Rate control often uses beta-blockers or calcium channel blockers.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Back Pain Red Flags
content.definition: Radiculopathy, cauda equina, infection, and malignancy features prompting urgent imaging.
content.summary: Cauda equina syndrome needs emergent MRI and decompression. Night pain suggests malignancy.

framing.relevance: Core musculoskeletal & rheumatology concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Red flags guide urgent spine imaging and referral for cauda equina, infection, and malignancy.

content.title: Bacterial and Fungal Skin Infections
content.definition: Impetigo, cellulitis, tinea, and candidiasis with topical and systemic antimicrobial therapy.
content.summary: Ringworm shows annular scaly plaques. Oral antifungals treat onychomycosis.

framing.relevance: Core dermatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Bipolar Disorder
content.definition: Manic and depressive episodes with mood stabilizers such as lithium and valproate preventing recurrence.
content.summary: Mania features elevated mood, decreased sleep, and risky behavior. Antidepressant monotherapy may trigger mania.

framing.relevance: Core psychiatry & behavioral health concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Bowel Obstruction
content.definition: Small and large bowel obstruction from adhesions, hernia, or malignancy with nasogastric decompression and surgery.
content.summary: Air-fluid levels on upright abdominal film. Closed-loop obstruction risks strangulation.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Breast Cancer Surgery
content.definition: Lumpectomy versus mastectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy and adjuvant therapy planning.
content.summary: Margins clear of tumor required for breast conservation. SLNB avoids full axillary dissection when negative.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Burns Management
content.definition: TBSA estimation, fluid resuscitation, eschar care, and referral criteria for burn centers.
content.summary: Parkland formula guides initial fluids. Circumferential burns may need escharotomy.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Calcium and Parathyroid Disorders
content.definition: Hyperparathyroidism and hypoparathyroidism altering serum calcium, phosphate, and bone turnover.
content.summary: Primary hyperparathyroidism causes stones, bones, groans, and moans. Hypocalcemia prolongs QT interval.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cardiomyopathies
content.definition: Dilated, hypertrophic, and restrictive patterns with genetic and acquired etiologies.
content.summary: HCM risks sudden death in young athletes. Restrictive disease stiffens ventricles impairing filling.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cervical Cancer Prevention
content.definition: HPV vaccination, Pap and HPV co-testing, and colposcopy for precancerous lesions.
content.summary: HPV 16 and 18 are high-risk oncogenic types. CIN management depends on grade and age.

framing.relevance: Core obstetrics & gynecology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Child and Adolescent Psychiatry
content.definition: ADHD, autism spectrum, and conduct disorders with developmental-appropriate assessment and therapy.
content.summary: ADHD treated with stimulants and behavioral interventions. Autism diagnosis uses standardized criteria.

framing.relevance: Core psychiatry & behavioral health concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cholecystitis and Cholelithiasis
content.definition: Gallstone disease with biliary colic versus acute cholecystitis treated with cholecystectomy.
content.summary: Murphy sign suggests cholecystitis. CBD stones may cause cholangitis and pancreatitis.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Chronic Kidney Disease Staging
content.definition: GFR and albuminuria categories guiding monitoring, complication prevention, and referral.
content.summary: CKD complications include anemia, bone disease, and cardiovascular risk. Dialysis considered at end stage.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cirrhosis and Portal Hypertension
content.definition: Chronic liver injury causing ascites, varices, encephalopathy, and hepatorenal syndrome.
content.summary: Nonselective beta-blockers reduce variceal bleed risk. Lactulose treats hepatic encephalopathy.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Coagulopathy and DIC
content.definition: Consumptive coagulopathy with thrombosis and bleeding in sepsis, malignancy, and obstetric catastrophes.
content.summary: DIC shows low fibrinogen and elevated D-dimer. Treat underlying trigger.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Colorectal Cancer Screening
content.definition: Colonoscopy and stool-based tests detecting adenomas and cancer in average-risk populations.
content.summary: Adenomatous polyps are premalignant. Screening begins age 45 in many guidelines.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Contraception Methods
content.definition: Hormonal, barrier, intrauterine, and permanent contraception with efficacy and contraindications.
content.summary: Copper IUD is highly effective nonhormonal option. Estrogen contraindicated in thrombophilia.

framing.relevance: Core obstetrics & gynecology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: COPD Clinical Management
content.definition: Progressive airflow limitation managed with bronchodilators, pulmonary rehab, and oxygen when indicated.
content.summary: GOLD staging guides therapy. Acute exacerbations often triggered by infection.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cushing Syndrome
content.definition: Excess cortisol from exogenous steroids, adrenal adenoma, or ACTH-dependent pituitary or ectopic sources.
content.summary: Dexamethasone suppression and ACTH level differentiate etiologies. Moon facies and striae are clinical clues.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Deep Vein Thrombosis and PE
content.definition: Venous thromboembolism diagnosis with Wells criteria, compression ultrasound, and anticoagulation duration.
content.summary: Virchow triad explains pathogenesis. Cancer-associated thrombosis may need extended anticoagulation.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: VTE is a leading complication after hip/knee arthroplasty and hip fracture surgery with orthopaedic-specific prophylaxis protocols.

content.title: Dementia Evaluation
content.definition: Progressive cognitive decline workup distinguishing Alzheimer, vascular, Lewy body, and reversible causes.
content.summary: Mini-Mental State Exam screens cognition. B12, TSH, and neuroimaging exclude treatable causes.

framing.relevance: Core neurology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Diabetes Mellitus Type 1 and Type 2
content.definition: Autoimmune insulin deficiency versus insulin resistance with distinct management and complication screening.
content.summary: DKA is type 1 emergency. Metformin is first-line in type 2 without contraindications.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Diabetic Ketoacidosis
content.definition: Insulin deficiency with hyperglycemia, ketosis, and anion gap acidosis requiring fluid and insulin therapy.
content.summary: Serum beta-hydroxybutyrate confirms ketosis. Potassium repletion critical during insulin treatment.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Drug Eruptions
content.definition: Morbilliform rash, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, and DRESS from medication hypersensitivity.
content.summary: SJS/TEN requires drug cessation and supportive burn-unit care. High mortality with extensive detachment.

framing.relevance: Core dermatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: ECG Interpretation Basics
content.definition: Rate, rhythm, axis, intervals, and ischemic ST-T wave pattern recognition.
content.summary: ST elevation localizes infarct territory. Wide QRS suggests ventricular origin or bundle branch block.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Eczema and Contact Dermatitis
content.definition: Atopic dermatitis and allergen-triggered dermatitis with moisturization, steroids, and allergen avoidance.
content.summary: Patch testing identifies contact allergens. Atopic triad includes asthma and allergic rhinitis.

framing.relevance: Core dermatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Potassium Disorders
content.definition: Hypokalemia and hyperkalemia with ECG changes and emergent membrane stabilization for severe hyperkalemia.
content.summary: Peaked T waves suggest hyperkalemia. Insulin and glucose shift potassium intracellularly temporizing.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Sodium Disorders
content.definition: Hyponatremia and hypernatremia reflecting water balance disturbances with osmotic correction principles.
content.summary: Acute hyponatremia corrected slowly to avoid osmotic demyelination. Hypernatremia implies water deficit.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: GERD and Peptic Ulcer Disease
content.definition: Acid reflux and mucosal ulceration managed with PPIs and H. pylori eradication when indicated.
content.summary: Alarm features warrant endoscopy. H. pylori quadruple therapy clears infection.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: GI Bleeding Evaluation
content.definition: Upper versus lower sources, hemodynamic resuscitation, and endoscopic or angiographic hemostasis.
content.summary: Melena suggests upper GI source. Rockall and Blatchford scores risk-stratify.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Gout and Hyperuricemia
content.definition: Monosodium urate crystal arthritis with acute colchicine or NSAIDs and long-term urate lowering.
content.summary: Podagra is classic first attack. Allopurinol lowers uric acid preventing recurrent attacks.

framing.relevance: Core musculoskeletal & rheumatology concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Crystal arthropathy mimics septic joint and affects foot and hand surgical planning.

content.title: Headache Red Flags and Secondary Causes
content.definition: Thunderclap, positional, and focal neurologic features prompting imaging for secondary headache.
content.summary: Subarachnoid hemorrhage presents sudden worst headache. Giant cell arteritis causes temporal headache in elderly.

framing.title_in_context: Headache Red Flags and Secondary Causes
framing.relevance: Core neurology concept for medical sciences.

hierarchy_location.topic: Headache Red Flags and Secondary Causes

content.title: Heart Failure Diagnosis and Management
content.definition: Reduced versus preserved ejection fraction with neurohormonal blockade and diuretic therapy.
content.summary: HFrEF benefits from ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, and MRAs. Volume overload warrants loop diuretics.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Hernia Repair Principles
content.definition: Inguinal and femoral hernias with reducibility, incarceration risk, and mesh repair techniques.
content.summary: Indirect inguinal hernia passes through internal ring. Femoral hernias have high incarceration risk.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: HIV/AIDS Management
content.definition: Antiretroviral therapy, opportunistic infection prophylaxis, and immune reconstitution monitoring.
content.summary: Undetectable viral load prevents sexual transmission. CD4 count guides OI prophylaxis thresholds.

framing.relevance: Core infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Hypertension Clinical Management
content.definition: Diagnosis, secondary causes, target organ damage, and stepwise pharmacotherapy.
content.summary: Resistant hypertension warrants secondary cause workup. Hypertensive emergency requires IV agents.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Infective Endocarditis
content.definition: Valve infection with vegetations, embolic phenomena, and blood culture-directed therapy.
content.summary: IV drug use predisposes to tricuspid involvement. Duke criteria integrate clinical and echo findings.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease
content.definition: Crohn disease and ulcerative colitis with immunomodulator and biologic therapy.
content.summary: UC is continuous colonic disease; Crohn is transmural with skip lesions. Biologics target TNF and integrins.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Interstitial Lung Disease
content.definition: Fibrotic and inflammatory parenchymal disorders reducing compliance and diffusing capacity.
content.summary: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis shows honeycombing on CT. Connective tissue disease may cause ILD.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Iron Deficiency Anemia
content.definition: Microcytic anemia from blood loss or inadequate intake with low ferritin and high TIBC.
content.summary: GI bleeding must be excluded in adult men and postmenopausal women. Iron repletion replaces stores.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
content.definition: Chronic abdominal pain with altered bowel habits diagnosed by criteria after excluding organic disease.
content.summary: Subtypes include IBS-C, IBS-D, and mixed. Dietary and neuromodulator therapies help symptoms.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Kidney Stones
content.definition: Calcium, uric acid, struvite, and cystine stones with imaging, analgesia, and prevention strategies.
content.summary: Calcium oxalate stones are most common. Thiazides reduce calcium stone recurrence.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Labor and Delivery
content.definition: Stages of labor, fetal monitoring, dystocia management, and operative delivery indications.
content.summary: Active labor progresses with cervical change. Category II tracing warrants close evaluation.

framing.relevance: Core obstetrics & gynecology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Leukemia Overview
content.definition: Acute and chronic leukemias of lymphoid and myeloid lineages with cytogenetic risk stratification.
content.summary: AML shows Auer rods. CML has BCR-ABL and responds to tyrosine kinase inhibitors.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Lipid Management and ASCVD Risk
content.definition: Statin therapy, risk calculators, and LDL targets for atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease prevention.
content.summary: High-intensity statins lower LDL and event rates. Familial hypercholesterolemia warrants early treatment.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Lung Cancer Evaluation
content.definition: Solitary nodule assessment, staging, and histologic subtypes guiding treatment.
content.summary: Smoking is major risk factor. Small cell versus non-small cell determines therapy approach.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Lymphoma Overview
content.definition: Hodgkin and non-Hodgkin lymphomas with nodal patterns, B symptoms, and chemoimmunotherapy regimens.
content.summary: Reed-Sternberg cells define Hodgkin lymphoma. DLBCL is common aggressive NHL.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Major Depressive Disorder
content.definition: Persistent depressed mood and anhedonia with biologic and psychosocial treatments including SSRIs and CBT.
content.summary: Screen for suicide risk at every visit. Combined therapy and medication often most effective.

framing.relevance: Core psychiatry & behavioral health concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Meningitis and Encephalitis
content.definition: Acute CNS infection presenting with fever, headache, and altered mental status; lumbar puncture guides empiric antibiotics and antivirals.
content.summary: Bacterial meningitis shows low glucose and high protein in CSF. HSV encephalitis needs acyclovir promptly. Organism-specific therapy follows culture.

knowledge_graph._placement_note: Merged meningitis_bacterial (Infectious Disease L2) with neurology presentation. Single clinical node spans both L2 subdivisions.

framing.relevance: Neurologic presentation and CSF interpretation; organism-specific empiric antibiotics, prophylaxis, and antivirals.

content.title: Multiple Sclerosis
content.definition: CNS demyelination with relapsing-remitting or progressive courses and MRI dissemination in time and space.
content.summary: Oligoclonal bands in CSF support diagnosis. Disease-modifying therapies reduce relapse rate.

framing.relevance: Core neurology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Nephritic Syndrome
content.definition: Hematuria, RBC casts, hypertension, and reduced GFR from glomerular inflammation.
content.summary: Poststreptococcal GN follows infection. RPGN requires urgent immunosuppression or plasmapheresis.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Nephrotic Syndrome
content.definition: Heavy proteinuria, hypoalbuminemia, edema, and hyperlipidemia from glomerular permeability defects.
content.summary: Minimal change disease common in children. Membranous nephropathy associates with PLA2R antibodies.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Obesity and Metabolic Syndrome
content.definition: Central adiposity with insulin resistance, dyslipidemia, and hypertension increasing cardiometabolic risk.
content.summary: Lifestyle intervention is foundation. GLP-1 agonists aid weight loss and glycemic control.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Anatomy
content.definition: Regional musculoskeletal anatomy and surgical approaches relevant to orthopaedic diagnosis and operative exposure.
content.summary: Bony, ligamentous, neurovascular, and muscular anatomy by region, plus standard surgical approaches.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Anatomy section; ABOS anatomy and approaches.

content.title: Basic Science
content.definition: Applied basic sciences supporting musculoskeletal diagnosis and treatment including biomechanics, imaging, implant science, and perioperative principles.
content.summary: Biomechanics, gait, musculoskeletal imaging, implant tribology, soft-tissue biology, fracture healing, and perioperative principles.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Basic Science section; ABOS General Principles.

content.title: Foot & Ankle
content.definition: Disorders of the foot and ankle including fracture, instability, deformity, arthritis, and diabetic foot disease.
content.summary: Ankle fracture, Achilles pathology, hindfoot and forefoot deformity, and Charcot arthropathy.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Foot & Ankle section; ABOS Foot and Ankle.

content.title: Hand
content.definition: Disorders of the hand and wrist including fracture, arthritis, tendon, nerve compression, and infection.
content.summary: Distal radius and carpal injuries, tendon repairs, nerve compression, Dupuytren, and hand infections.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Hand section; ABOS Hand.

content.title: Knee & Sports
content.definition: Athletic and degenerative knee injuries and broader sports medicine including ligament, meniscal, cartilage, and patellofemoral disorders.
content.summary: Knee ligament and meniscal injury, patellofemoral disorders, cartilage restoration, and medical sports medicine.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Knee & Sports section; ABOS Sports Medicine.

content.title: Pathology
content.definition: Benign and malignant tumors of bone and soft tissue plus metabolic and infectious musculoskeletal pathology, including staging, biopsy, and limb salvage.
content.summary: Tumor evaluation, primary bone malignancy, soft-tissue sarcoma, metastatic disease, metabolic bone disease, and musculoskeletal infection.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Pathology section; ABOS Oncology and pathology.

content.title: Pediatrics
content.definition: Musculoskeletal disorders in children including developmental hip conditions, physeal trauma, spine deformity, and neuromuscular disease.
content.summary: DDH, SCFE, Perthes, clubfoot, physeal fractures, paediatric spine, CP, and skeletal dysplasias.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Pediatrics section; ABOS Pediatrics.

content.title: Recon
content.definition: Elective joint replacement and revision surgery for end-stage arthropathy of the hip and knee, plus reconstruction basic science.
content.summary: Hip and knee arthropathy, arthroplasty, bearing surfaces, wear and osteolysis, PJI, and periprosthetic fracture.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Recon section; ABOS Adult Reconstruction.

content.title: Shoulder & Elbow
content.definition: Non-traumatic and post-traumatic disorders of the shoulder and elbow including instability, cuff pathology, arthritis, and arthroplasty.
content.summary: Shoulder instability, rotator cuff, arthroplasty, elbow arthritis, and throwing-athlete pathology.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Shoulder & Elbow section; ABOS Shoulder and Elbow.

content.title: Spine
content.definition: Degenerative, deformity, infectious, neoplastic, and traumatic disorders of the vertebral column and spinal cord.
content.summary: Cervical, thoracic, and lumbar pathology, spinal deformity, trauma, tumors, and fusion principles.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Spine section; ABOS Adult Spine.

content.title: Trauma
content.definition: Acute musculoskeletal injuries including fractures, dislocations, polytrauma, and complications of traumatic injury.
content.summary: Fracture principles, regional trauma by extremity, pelvis and acetabulum, and damage-control orthopaedics.

framing.relevance: OrthoBullets Trauma section; ABOS Adult Trauma.

content.title: Osteoarthritis
content.definition: Cartilage loss and osteophyte formation causing joint pain managed with exercise, NSAIDs, and injections.
content.summary: Weight loss reduces knee OA symptoms. Joint replacement for end-stage disease.

framing.relevance: Core musculoskeletal & rheumatology concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: General OA management precedes surgical arthroplasty decisions in hip and knee reconstruction.

content.title: Osteoporosis Management
content.definition: DEXA screening, fracture risk assessment, and bisphosphonate or anabolic therapy.
content.summary: Fragility fractures occur from low trauma. Calcium and vitamin D support bone health.

framing.relevance: Core musculoskeletal & rheumatology concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Bone health optimization reduces fragility fracture and periprosthetic fracture risk. Primary home for metabolic bone disease in orthopaedic context (merged from metabolic_bone L3).

content.title: Parkinson Disease
content.definition: Bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor, and postural instability from nigrostriatal dopamine loss.
content.summary: Levodopa is most effective symptomatic therapy. Deep brain stimulation helps motor fluctuations.

framing.relevance: Core neurology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pelvic Inflammatory Disease
content.definition: Ascending infection causing salpingitis with antibiotic regimens covering gonorrhea and chlamydia.
content.summary: PID risks ectopic pregnancy and infertility. Partner treatment prevents reinfection.

framing.relevance: Core obstetrics & gynecology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pericardial Disease
content.definition: Acute pericarditis, effusion, tamponade, and constrictive physiology affecting diastolic filling.
content.summary: Beck triad suggests tamponade. Constriction shows pericardial knock and Kussmaul sign.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Peripheral Neuropathy
content.definition: Length-dependent axonal and demyelinating neuropathies from diabetes, toxins, and nutritional deficiency.
content.summary: Stocking-glove sensory loss is typical. EMG and nerve conduction distinguish axonal from demyelinating.

framing.relevance: Core neurology concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Systemic neuropathy contributes to foot ulceration, Charcot, and fall risk in elderly orthopaedic patients.

content.title: Pituitary Disorders
content.definition: Adenomas causing hormone hypersecretion or hypopituitarism with visual field defects when suprasellar.
content.summary: Prolactinoma treated with dopamine agonists. Acromegaly from GH excess causes enlargement features.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pleural Effusion Evaluation
content.definition: Transudative versus exudative effusions by Light criteria with diagnostic thoracentesis.
content.summary: Exudates suggest inflammation or malignancy. Large effusions cause dyspnea requiring drainage.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pneumonia Clinical Approach
content.definition: Community and hospital-acquired pneumonia with severity scoring and empiric antibiotics.
content.summary: CURB-65 estimates mortality risk. Atypical coverage considered in outpatient regimens.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Postoperative Fever
content.definition: Timing-based differential including atelectasis, wound infection, UTI, DVT, and drug fever.
content.summary: Wind-water-wound-walk-wonder mnemonic organizes causes by postoperative day.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Postpartum Complications
content.definition: Hemorrhage, infection, depression, and thromboembolism in the postpartum period.
content.summary: Uterine atony is leading cause of postpartum hemorrhage. Oxytocin and massage are first-line.

framing.relevance: Core obstetrics & gynecology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Prenatal Care and Screening
content.definition: Trimester visits, ultrasound anatomy survey, and screening for aneuploidy and gestational diabetes.
content.summary: Folic acid prevents neural tube defects. Rh incompatibility managed with anti-D immunoglobulin.

framing.relevance: Core obstetrics & gynecology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Preoperative Assessment
content.definition: Cardiopulmonary risk stratification, medication management, and fasting guidelines before surgery.
content.summary: RCRI estimates cardiac risk. Beta-blockers continued perioperatively when already indicated.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: General preoperative assessment extended by orthopaedic-specific optimization (nutrition, smoking, inflammatory arthritis, prehabilitation).

content.title: Psoriasis
content.definition: Plaque psoriasis and variants with topical, phototherapy, and systemic immunomodulator options.
content.summary: Nail pitting and psoriatic arthritis associate with skin disease. Biologics target IL-17 and TNF.

framing.relevance: Core dermatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pulmonary Embolism
content.definition: Thromboembolic occlusion of pulmonary arteries causing hypoxemia and right heart strain.
content.summary: Wells score and D-dimer guide workup. Anticoagulation is cornerstone of treatment.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Rheumatoid Arthritis
content.definition: Symmetric polyarthritis with erosions treated early with DMARDs and biologics to prevent joint damage.
content.summary: RF and anti-CCP support diagnosis. Methotrexate is anchor DMARD therapy.

framing.relevance: Core musculoskeletal & rheumatology concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Inflammatory arthritis drives upper extremity deformity, arthroplasty timing, and perioperative biologic management.

content.title: Seizure and Epilepsy
content.definition: Provoked seizures versus epilepsy syndromes with antiepileptic drug selection by seizure type.
content.summary: Status epilepticus is neurologic emergency. Absence seizures show 3 Hz spike-wave on EEG.

framing.relevance: Core neurology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Sepsis and Septic Shock
content.definition: Dysregulated host response to infection with organ dysfunction requiring early antibiotics and resuscitation.
content.summary: qSOFA and SOFA scores identify severity. Hour-1 bundle emphasizes cultures and antibiotics.

framing.relevance: Core infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Sickle Cell Disease
content.definition: Hemoglobin S polymerization causing vaso-occlusive pain, hemolysis, and organ damage.
content.summary: Hydroxyurea increases fetal hemoglobin. Splenic autoinfarction increases infection risk.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Skin Cancer Screening
content.definition: ABCDE criteria for melanoma and management of basal and squamous cell carcinoma.
content.summary: Melanoma depth on Breslow scale guides prognosis. Wide local excision is primary treatment.

framing.relevance: Core dermatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Skin and Soft Tissue Infections
content.definition: Cellulitis, abscess, necrotizing fasciitis with drainage, antibiotics, and surgical debridement when urgent.
content.summary: Necrotizing infection needs immediate surgery. MRSA coverage considered in community abscess.

framing.relevance: Core infectious disease concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Cellulitis and necrotizing infection overlap with septic arthritis and osteomyelitis workup in orthopaedic practice.
applications: Septic arthritis vs gout vs cellulitis

content.title: Obstructive Sleep Apnea
content.definition: Recurrent upper airway collapse during sleep causing hypoxemia, fragmentation, and daytime somnolence.
content.summary: CPAP is first-line therapy. OSA associates with hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

framing.relevance: Core pulmonology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Solid Tumor Management Principles
content.definition: Surgery, radiation, systemic therapy, and staging informing multidisciplinary cancer care.
content.summary: TNM staging guides prognosis. Adjuvant therapy reduces recurrence after curative resection.

framing.relevance: Core hematology & oncology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute Stroke Management
content.definition: Ischemic stroke thrombolysis and thrombectomy windows with blood pressure and glucose control.
content.summary: tPA within 4.5 hours for eligible ischemic stroke. NIHSS quantifies deficit severity.

framing.relevance: Core neurology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Syncope Evaluation
content.definition: Reflex, cardiac, orthostatic, and neurologic causes distinguished by history and provocative testing.
content.summary: Cardiac syncope carries highest mortality risk. Carotid sinus massage assesses reflex syncope.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
content.definition: Multisystem autoimmunity with malar rash, serositis, nephritis, and ANA-associated antibodies.
content.summary: Lupus nephritis requires immunosuppression. Hydroxychloroquine reduces flares and mortality.

framing.relevance: Core musculoskeletal & rheumatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Hypothyroidism and Hyperthyroidism
content.definition: TSH-centered diagnosis with levothyroxine replacement or antithyroid drugs, radioiodine, or surgery.
content.summary: Primary hypothyroidism shows high TSH and low free T4. Graves disease causes hyperthyroidism with autoantibodies.

framing.relevance: Core endocrinology & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Thyroid Surgery Complications
content.definition: Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury, hypocalcemia from parathyroid damage, and hematoma airway compromise.
content.summary: Hoarseness suggests nerve injury. Perioperative calcium monitoring detects hypoparathyroidism.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Tick-Borne Illnesses
content.definition: Lyme disease, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, and ehrlichiosis with geographic and exposure history.
content.summary: Erythema migrans is pathognomonic early Lyme. Doxycycline treats many tick-borne rickettsial diseases.

framing.relevance: Core infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Trauma Primary Survey
content.definition: ABCDE approach stabilizing airway, breathing, circulation, disability, and exposure in injured patients.
content.summary: Massive transfusion protocol for hemorrhagic shock. FAST exam detects intraperitoneal blood.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Travel-Related Infections
content.definition: Malaria, typhoid, and travelers diarrhea prevention and treatment by destination risk.
content.summary: Malaria prophylaxis depends on resistance patterns. Oral rehydration treats most travelers diarrhea.

framing.relevance: Core infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Tuberculosis
content.definition: Latent versus active TB with RIPE therapy and public health contact tracing.
content.summary: Latent TB treated with isoniazid or rifapentine regimens. MDR-TB requires prolonged multidrug therapy.

framing.relevance: Core infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Urinary Tract Infection
content.definition: Cystitis versus pyelonephritis with empiric antibiotics guided by resistance patterns.
content.summary: Uncomplicated cystitis treated short course in women. Fever and flank pain suggest pyelonephritis.

framing.relevance: Core nephrology & fluid balance concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Valvular Heart Disease
content.definition: Stenosis and regurgitation of aortic and mitral valves with hemodynamic consequences.
content.summary: Aortic stenosis causes syncope, angina, and heart failure. Mitral regurgitation volume overloads left ventricle.

framing.relevance: Core cardiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Vasculitis Syndromes
content.definition: Small, medium, and large vessel vasculitides with organ-specific manifestations and immunosuppressive treatment.
content.summary: Giant cell arteritis causes headache and vision loss. ANCA-associated vasculitis affects kidneys and lungs.

framing.relevance: Core musculoskeletal & rheumatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Viral Hepatitis
content.definition: Hepatitis A, B, C, D, and E with serologic patterns and antiviral or supportive therapy.
content.summary: HBsAg indicates active B infection. Direct-acting antivirals cure most hepatitis C.

framing.relevance: Core gastroenterology & hepatology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Surgical Wound Healing
content.definition: Primary, secondary, and tertiary closure with factors impairing healing including diabetes and infection.
content.summary: Tension-free closure reduces dehiscence. Prophylactic antibiotics indicated for dirty-contaminated cases.

framing.relevance: Core surgery & perioperative care concept for medical sciences. Orthopaedic context: Surgical wound complications and SSI prevention bundles for arthroplasty, open fractures, and flap coverage.

content.title: Renal Acid-Base Balance
content.definition: Bicarbonate reabsorption, ammonium excretion, and titratable acid eliminating nonvolatile acid load.
content.summary: Renal compensation for respiratory disorders is slower but sustained. Distal RTA reflects specific transporter defects.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute Kidney Injury Pathology
content.definition: Prerenal, intrinsic, and postrenal mechanisms with tubular necrosis and vascular causes.
content.summary: ATN shows muddy brown casts. Bilateral ureteral obstruction is a postrenal cause.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Amino Acid Metabolism and Urea Cycle
content.definition: Transamination, deamination, and hepatic urea synthesis disposing of nitrogen.
content.summary: Hyperammonemia from urea cycle defects causes encephalopathy. Maple syrup urine disease blocks branched-chain degradation.

framing.title_in_context: Amino Acid Metabolism and Urea Cycle
framing.relevance: Core biochemistry & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

hierarchy_location.topic: Amino Acid Metabolism and Urea Cycle

content.title: Anemia Classification and Pathology
content.definition: Microcytic, normocytic, and macrocytic anemias with hemolytic and production failure mechanisms.
content.summary: Iron deficiency is microcytic hypochromic. B12 or folate deficiency causes megaloblastic macrocytosis.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Antiarrhythmic Drug Classes
content.definition: Vaughan Williams classification targeting sodium, beta, potassium, and calcium channels in cardiac tissue.
content.summary: Class I agents block Na channels prolonging depolarization. Class III prolong repolarization via K blockade.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Anticoagulant and Antiplatelet Agents
content.definition: Warfarin, heparin, direct oral anticoagulants, and antiplatelet drugs interrupting clotting cascades.
content.summary: Warfarin requires INR monitoring and vitamin K reversal. Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia is an immune complication.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Antihypertensive Drug Mechanisms
content.definition: Agents lowering blood pressure via diuresis, RAAS blockade, vasodilation, or cardiac depression.
content.summary: ACE inhibitors cause cough and angioedema. Thiazides are first-line in many hypertension guidelines.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Antimicrobial Mechanisms and Resistance
content.definition: Drug classes inhibiting cell wall, protein, nucleic acid, or folate synthesis with resistance mechanisms.
content.summary: Beta-lactams require active cell wall synthesis. Efflux pumps and modifying enzymes confer resistance.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Atherosclerosis Pathogenesis
content.definition: Endothelial dysfunction, lipid accumulation, inflammatory infiltration, and plaque rupture in arterial disease.
content.summary: Stable plaques have thick fibrous caps. Rupture exposes thrombogenic lipid core causing acute coronary events.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Autonomic Nervous System Organization
content.definition: Sympathetic and parasympathetic divisions with ganglionic synapses and target organ effects.
content.summary: Sympathetic activation increases heart rate and bronchodilation. Parasympathetic stimulates digestion.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Adrenergic Pharmacology
content.definition: Alpha and beta receptor agonists and antagonists affecting cardiovascular and bronchial tone.
content.summary: Beta-blockers reduce heart rate and contractility. Alpha-1 blockade causes orthostatic hypotension.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cholinergic Pharmacology
content.definition: Drugs modulating acetylcholine synthesis, release, receptor binding, and acetylcholinesterase activity.
content.summary: Muscarinic agonists increase secretions and motility. Anticholinesterases treat myasthenia but risk cholinergic crisis.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Bias and Confounding
content.definition: Selection, information, and confounding biases threatening validity with stratification and matching remedies.
content.summary: Confounding distorts exposure-outcome association. Randomization balances known and unknown confounders.

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Blood Pressure Regulation
content.definition: Baroreceptor reflex, renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system, and autonomic control of vascular tone.
content.summary: Baroreceptor resetting occurs in chronic hypertension. RAAS integrates renal and vascular responses.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Body Planes and Regions
content.definition: Standard anatomic planes (sagittal, coronal, transverse) and regional nomenclature used to describe structure location.
content.summary: Planes and directional terms localize injuries and surgical approaches. Regional anatomy groups structures for clinical examination.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cardiac Membrane Potential
content.definition: Atrial and ventricular myocyte ion fluxes producing resting potential, phase 0–4 currents, and pacemaker automaticity.
content.summary: Cardiac action potential phases explain antiarrhythmic drug classes. Pacemaker cells drive intrinsic heart rate.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cardiac Output and Frank-Starling Law
content.definition: Stroke volume and heart rate determining cardiac output with preload-dependent sarcomere length-tension relationships.
content.summary: Starling mechanism matches venous return to output. Heart failure shifts the ventricular function curve.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cardiac Physiology
content.definition: Integrated function of the heart as a pump: rhythmic contraction, conduction, preload/afterload, and cardiac output regulation in the human circulation.
content.summary: Cardiac physiology links electrophysiology to hemodynamics. Starling law and autonomic tone determine output in health and heart failure.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Human cardiac physiology owned by medicine_preclinical. Biology carries only general circulatory principles as context.

framing.relevance: Step 1 cardiovascular foundation — ECG, murmurs, heart failure.
General vertebrate circulatory design — thin context only.

content.title: Heart and Great Vessels Anatomy
content.definition: Chambers, valves, coronary circulation, and major arterial-venous connections of the thoracic cardiovascular system.
content.summary: Cardiac chamber anatomy explains murmur locations and conduction pathways. Coronary dominance affects ischemia patterns.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cardiovascular Embryology
content.definition: Heart tube looping, septation, and outflow tract remodeling forming four-chamber circulation.
content.summary: Conotruncal anomalies link to neural crest migration. Septation defects explain left-to-right shunts at birth.

framing.relevance: Core embryology & development concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cell Injury and Adaptation
content.definition: Reversible injury, necrosis, apoptosis, and hypertrophy-atrophy-metaplasia responses to stress.
content.summary: Ischemia causes ATP depletion and reversible swelling before necrosis. Apoptosis is programmed and regulated.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cell Junctions and Adhesion
content.definition: Tight, adherens, desmosomal, and gap junctions organizing tissue barriers and intercellular communication.
content.summary: Tight junction integrity maintains epithelial polarity. Desmosome autoimmunity causes blistering skin disease.

framing.relevance: Core histology & cell ultrastructure concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cell-Mediated Immunity
content.definition: CD4 helper and CD8 cytotoxic T cell activation, cytokine networks, and intracellular pathogen control.
content.summary: Th1 cells activate macrophages for intracellular bacteria. CTLs kill virus-infected cells.

framing.relevance: Core immunology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cell Organelles and Ultrastructure
content.definition: Membrane-bound organelles and cytoskeletal elements visible at electron microscopic resolution.
content.summary: Organelle defects cause inherited metabolic disease. Mitochondrial ultrastructure links to oxidative phosphorylation capacity.

framing.relevance: Core histology & cell ultrastructure concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Chemotherapy Cell-Cycle Specificity
content.definition: Antineoplastic agents targeting S, M, or G phases versus cycle-nonspecific DNA alkylators.
content.summary: Methotrexate is S-phase specific. Combination regimens balance efficacy and overlapping toxicity.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Chromosomal Abnormalities
content.definition: Aneuploidy, translocations, and microdeletions causing syndromic disease.
content.summary: Trisomy 21 causes Down syndrome. Philadelphia chromosome t(9;22) defines CML.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: CNS Sedatives and Antidepressants
content.definition: Benzodiazepines, barbiturates, SSRIs, and related agents modulating inhibitory and monoaminergic signaling.
content.summary: Benzodiazepines enhance GABA-A chloride influx. SSRIs increase synaptic serotonin via reuptake blockade.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: CNS Stroke and Hemorrhage
content.definition: Ischemic infarction with liquefactive necrosis versus intraparenchymal and subarachnoid hemorrhage.
content.summary: Embolic strokes are sudden and maximal at onset. Berry aneurysm rupture causes subarachnoid hemorrhage.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Coagulation Disorders
content.definition: Inherited and acquired defects in platelet function, coagulation factors, and fibrinolysis causing bleeding or thrombosis.
content.summary: Hemophilia A is factor VIII deficiency. DIC consumes clotting factors and platelets.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Collagen Synthesis and Disorders
content.definition: Post-translational hydroxylation, glycosylation, and cross-linking of procollagen with heritable defects.
content.summary: Osteogenesis imperfecta reflects type I collagen defects. Ehlers-Danlos syndromes involve collagen processing.

framing.relevance: Core biochemistry & metabolism concept for medical sciences.
Bone matrix biology, collagen cross-linking, and metabolic bone disorders underpin fracture healing, implant osseointegration, and skeletal dysplasias.

content.title: Connective Tissue Matrix
content.definition: Extracellular matrix components including collagen, elastin, and ground substance produced by fibroblasts and related cells.
content.summary: Matrix composition determines tissue strength and repair. Collagen types correlate with scar formation and heritable disorders.

framing.relevance: Core histology & cell ultrastructure concept for medical sciences.
Collagen and matrix biology relate to ligament healing, tendon repair, and skeletal dysplasias.

content.title: COPD and Emphysema Pathology
content.definition: Chronic bronchitis with mucous gland hypertrophy versus alveolar wall destruction in emphysema.
content.summary: Centriacinar emphysema associates with smoking. Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency causes panacinar disease.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cranial Nerve Functions
content.definition: Twelve cranial nerves with sensory, motor, and autonomic components tested clinically.
content.summary: CN III palsy causes ptosis and down-out eye. Facial nerve lesions distinguish upper versus lower motor patterns.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Psychological Defense Mechanisms
content.definition: Unconscious strategies such as denial, projection, and sublimation reducing anxiety.
content.summary: Mature defenses include humor and altruism. Immature defenses include acting out and splitting.

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Diabetes Mellitus Complications
content.definition: Microvascular and macrovascular damage including nephropathy, retinopathy, neuropathy, and accelerated atherosclerosis.
content.summary: Nonenzymatic glycation underlies microvascular injury. Diabetic ketoacidosis reflects insulin deficiency.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Diabetes Pharmacotherapy
content.definition: Insulin preparations and oral agents increasing secretion, sensitivity, or reducing hepatic glucose output.
content.summary: Metformin lowers hepatic gluconeogenesis. Sulfonylureas stimulate insulin release risking hypoglycemia.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: DNA Repair and Cancer Predisposition
content.definition: Mismatch, nucleotide excision, and double-strand break repair defects increasing mutation and malignancy risk.
content.summary: BRCA mutations impair homologous recombination. Lynch syndrome reflects mismatch repair defects.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Drug Elimination Kinetics
content.definition: First-order clearance processes removing drug proportional to plasma concentration with characteristic half-life.
content.summary: Most drugs follow first-order elimination. Clearance and half-life determine dosing intervals.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Cytochrome P450 Drug Metabolism
content.definition: Hepatic phase I oxidation-reduction reactions primarily catalyzed by CYP isoforms with inducible activity.
content.summary: CYP induction accelerates metabolism of other substrates. Genetic polymorphisms alter drug levels.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Enteric Gram-Negative Rods
content.definition: Lactose fermenters and nonfermenters causing UTI, gastroenteritis, and sepsis.
content.summary: E. coli UTI ascends from perineum. Salmonella and Shigella cause dysenteric syndromes.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Epithelial Tissue Types
content.definition: Simple and stratified epithelia classified by cell shape and layering with specialized surface modifications.
content.summary: Epithelial classification predicts carcinoma origin and barrier function. Cilia and microvilli reflect organ-specific roles.

framing.relevance: Core histology & cell ultrastructure concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Fatty Acid Synthesis and Beta-Oxidation
content.definition: Cytosolic lipogenesis versus mitochondrial beta-oxidation supplying acetyl-CoA and ketone bodies.
content.summary: Carnitine shuttle defects impair long-chain oxidation. Ketogenesis rises during prolonged fasting.

framing.relevance: Core biochemistry & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Fetal Circulation and Neonatal Transition
content.definition: Placental gas exchange, shunts (foramen ovale, ductus arteriosus), and postnatal closure.
content.summary: Persistent fetal shunts cause cyanotic disease. Ductus-dependent lesions require prostaglandin management.

framing.relevance: Core embryology & development concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Fungal and Parasitic Overview
content.definition: Eukaryotic pathogens with distinctive morphology, life cycles, and host tissue tropism.
content.summary: Dimorphic fungi switch forms with temperature. Plasmodium life cycle involves mosquito and human hosts.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Gas Exchange and Ventilation-Perfusion Matching
content.definition: Oxygen and carbon dioxide diffusion across the alveolar-capillary membrane with regional ventilation–perfusion ratios determining arterial blood gas composition.
content.summary: V/Q mismatch is the dominant mechanism of hypoxemia. Dead space and shunt physiology explain the A–a gradient in pulmonary disease.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Merged respiratory_gas_exchange (biology) with gas_exchange_and_ventilation_perfusion (medicine_preclinical).

framing.relevance: Pulmonary pathophysiology and ABG interpretation.
General principles of diffusion across respiratory surfaces.

content.title: Gastrointestinal Embryology
content.definition: Foregut rotation, midgut herniation, and hindgut partitioning with vitelline duct regression.
content.summary: Rotation errors cause volvulus and malrotation. Meckel diverticulum reflects persistent vitelline duct.

framing.relevance: Core embryology & development concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Gastrointestinal Tract Anatomy
content.definition: Oral cavity through anal canal including foregut-midgut-hindgut derivatives and associated vasculature.
content.summary: GI segmental blood supply predicts ischemia territories. Portosystemic anastomoses explain variceal bleeding.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Gene Expression Regulation
content.definition: Transcription factors, enhancers, epigenetic marks, and RNA processing controlling protein output.
content.summary: Methylation silences promoters. Alternative splicing generates protein diversity from one gene.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Germ Layer Derivatives
content.definition: Ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm giving rise to organ systems during early organogenesis.
content.summary: Germ layer origin predicts congenital malformation patterns. Neural crest derivatives span diverse tissues.

framing.relevance: Core embryology & development concept for medical sciences.

content.title: GI Motility and Secretion
content.definition: Peristalsis, sphincter tone, and exocrine-endocrine secretions coordinated by neural and hormonal signals.
content.summary: Migrating motor complexes clear fasting stomach. Secretin and CCK orchestrate digestive enzyme release.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Glomerular Disease Patterns
content.definition: Nephritic versus nephrotic syndromes with proliferative, membranous, and crescentic histologic patterns.
content.summary: Nephrotic syndrome features heavy proteinuria and edema. Crescentic GN rapidly reduces GFR.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Healthcare-Associated Infections
content.definition: Nosocomial pathogens, device-related biofilms, and infection control measures in hospitals.
content.summary: Catheter-related UTI is common nosocomial infection. MRSA and VRE require contact precautions.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Healthcare Delivery and Quality
content.definition: Primary-secondary-tertiary prevention, quality improvement cycles, and patient safety systems.
content.summary: Primary prevention avoids disease onset. PDSA cycles drive continuous quality improvement.

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Heme Synthesis and Porphyrias
content.definition: Mitochondrial and cytosolic steps forming protoporphyrin IX and heme with enzymatic block porphyrias.
content.summary: ALA and PBG accumulate in acute intermittent porphyria. Lead inhibits ALA dehydratase and ferrochelatase.

framing.relevance: Core biochemistry & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Hepatitis and Cirrhosis
content.definition: Viral and toxic liver injury progressing to fibrosis, nodular regeneration, and portal hypertension.
content.summary: Cirrhosis disrupts architecture with regenerative nodules. Ascites reflects portal hypertension and hypoalbuminemia.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: HIV and Immunodeficiency Viruses
content.definition: CD4 tropism, reverse transcription, and progressive immunodeficiency with opportunistic disease.
content.summary: HIV fusion requires CD4 and co-receptors. AIDS defines severe CD4 depletion with defining illnesses.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Humoral Immunity and Antibodies
content.definition: B cell differentiation to plasma cells producing IgM, IgG, IgA, IgE, and IgD with effector functions.
content.summary: IgG crosses placenta providing neonatal immunity. IgE mediates type I hypersensitivity.

framing.relevance: Core immunology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Genomic Imprinting and Uniparental Disomy
content.definition: Parent-of-origin-specific gene expression and chromosome pair from one parent causing disease.
content.summary: Prader-Willi versus Angelman reflect 15q deletion parent of origin. UPD can mimic deletions.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Acute and Chronic Inflammation
content.definition: Vascular and cellular events with neutrophil and macrophage recruitment resolving or persisting as chronicity.
content.summary: Acute inflammation features exudate and neutrophils. Granulomas characterize chronic immune activation.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.
Inflammatory cascades drive postoperative swelling, periprosthetic osteolysis, aseptic loosening, and synovitis in arthropathies. Osteolysis is a major arthroplasty failure mode.

content.title: Measures of Disease Frequency
content.definition: Incidence, prevalence, mortality, and morbidity quantifying population health burden.
content.summary: Prevalence rises with chronic disease duration. Incidence captures new cases over time.

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Medical Ethics Principles
content.definition: Autonomy, beneficence, nonmaleficence, and justice applied to consent, confidentiality, and resource allocation.
content.summary: Informed consent requires capacity, disclosure, and voluntariness. Justice addresses fair distribution of care.

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.
Informed consent and disclosure apply to high-risk orthopaedic procedures including amputation and tumor surgery.

content.title: Membrane Transport Ultrastructure
content.definition: Channel, carrier, and vesicular transport machinery at the plasma membrane and organelle membranes.
content.summary: Transport protein localization explains secretory versus absorptive epithelia. Endocytosis pathways mediate receptor turnover.

framing.relevance: Core histology & cell ultrastructure concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Mendelian Inheritance Patterns
content.definition: Autosomal dominant, autosomal recessive, and X-linked transmission with pedigree interpretation.
content.summary: Dominant disorders often involve haploinsufficiency. Consanguinity increases recessive disease risk.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: MHC and Antigen Presentation
content.definition: Class I presentation to CD8 T cells and class II to CD4 T cells linking innate detection to adaptive response.
content.summary: MHC I presents endogenous peptides. MHC II presents exogenous antigens from phagosomes.

framing.relevance: Core immunology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Molecular Diagnostic Techniques
content.definition: PCR, sequencing, microarrays, and CRISPR-based assays detecting mutations and pathogens.
content.summary: PCR amplifies specific DNA for rapid diagnosis. Next-generation sequencing enables panel testing.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Motivation and Stages of Change
content.definition: Transtheoretical model from precontemplation through maintenance guiding health behavior counseling.
content.summary: Matching interventions to stage improves adherence. Relapse is expected in behavior change.

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Motor Systems and Movement
content.definition: Upper and lower motor neuron hierarchy, cerebellar coordination, and basal ganglia motor loops.
content.summary: UMN lesions cause spasticity and hyperreflexia. Basal ganglia disorders alter initiation and tone.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Muscle Contraction
content.definition: Sliding-filament mechanism in which actin–myosin cross-bridge cycling, calcium-dependent thin-filament regulation, and ATP hydrolysis shorten sarcomeres in skeletal muscle.
content.summary: Excitation–contraction coupling links membrane depolarization to calcium release. Fiber type and motor unit recruitment grade force output.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Merged muscle_contraction (biology) and skeletal_muscle_contraction (medicine_preclinical).

framing.relevance: Human musculoskeletal and neuromuscular pharmacology foundation.
Conserved sliding-filament mechanism across animals.

content.title: Muscle Tissue Histology
content.definition: Skeletal, cardiac, and smooth muscle microscopic structure including sarcomeres and intercalated discs.
content.summary: Muscle histology distinguishes rhabdomyolysis from cardiomyopathy. Intercalated discs enable coordinated cardiac contraction.

framing.relevance: Core histology & cell ultrastructure concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Musculoskeletal System Overview
content.definition: Bones, joints, muscles, and connective tissues that provide support, protection, and movement.
content.summary: Musculoskeletal anatomy links palpable landmarks to underlying pathology. Origin-insertion relationships predict movement deficits.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.
Foundational MSK anatomy and physiology prerequisite for all orthopaedic subspecialty nodes.

content.title: Mycobacteria and Acid-Fast Organisms
content.definition: Lipid-rich cell walls requiring acid-fast staining with slow growth and granulomatous host response.
content.summary: M. tuberculosis forms caseating granulomas. MAC infects advanced HIV patients.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Myocardial Infarction Pathology
content.definition: Coagulative necrosis with wavefront progression, reperfusion injury, and healing by granulation tissue.
content.summary: Transmural infarcts involve full wall thickness. Troponin rise reflects cardiomyocyte necrosis.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Benign and Malignant Neoplasia
content.definition: Uncontrolled clonal growth with dysplasia, invasion, and metastasis distinguishing malignancy.
content.summary: Benign tumors are encapsulated and noninvasive. Carcinoma in situ lacks basement membrane breach.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Nervous System Gross Organization
content.definition: Central and peripheral nervous system divisions, meninges, ventricles, and major fiber tracts.
content.summary: Gross neuroanatomy localizes lesions by motor-sensory level. Meningeal layers explain epidural versus subdural collections.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Nervous Tissue Histology
content.definition: Neurons, glia, and myelin with regional cytoarchitecture of gray and white matter.
