content.summary: Glial proportions explain glioma categories. Myelin histology underlies demyelinating disease pathology.

framing.relevance: Core histology & cell ultrastructure concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Brain Regions and Functional Anatomy
content.definition: Cerebral lobes, basal ganglia, thalamus, brainstem, and cerebellum with major functional roles.
content.summary: Frontal lobe governs executive function. Brainstem houses vital cardiorespiratory centers.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Neurodegenerative Disease Pathology
content.definition: Protein aggregation disorders including Alzheimer, Parkinson, and prion diseases with selective neuronal loss.
content.summary: Alzheimer shows amyloid plaques and neurofibrillary tangles. Lewy bodies characterize Parkinson disease.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Neurulation and Neural Tube Formation
content.definition: Folding of neural plate into neural tube with closure defects producing spina bifida and anencephaly.
content.summary: Neural tube closure timing explains folic acid prevention. Level of defect correlates with neurologic deficit.

framing.relevance: Core embryology & development concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Normal Flora and Opportunistic Infection
content.definition: Commensal organisms preventing colonization versus overgrowth causing disease when barriers fail.
content.summary: C. difficile colitis follows antibiotic disruption. Immunocompromise enables opportunistic infections.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: NSAID and Opioid Pharmacology
content.definition: COX inhibition for inflammation and analgesia versus mu-receptor opioid effects and dependence.
content.summary: NSAIDs risk GI ulceration and renal impairment. Opioids cause respiratory depression and constipation.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Nucleotide Synthesis and Purine Salvage
content.definition: De novo and salvage pathways for purines and pyrimidines with feedback inhibition.
content.summary: HGPRT deficiency causes Lesch-Nyhan syndrome. Antimetabolites target nucleotide synthesis in cancer.

framing.relevance: Core biochemistry & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Nutrient Absorption Physiology
content.definition: Enzymatic digestion of carbohydrates, proteins, and lipids with absorption of monomers and micronutrients across the gastrointestinal epithelium into portal blood and lymph.
content.summary: Transporter-mediated uptake and bile-dependent fat absorption underpin malabsorption syndromes. Lactase deficiency illustrates osmotic diarrhea.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Merged digestive_absorption (biology) with nutrient_absorption_physiology (medicine_preclinical).

framing.relevance: GI pathophysiology and malabsorption workups.
General nutrient uptake across animal digestive systems.

content.title: Oxygen-Hemoglobin Dissociation
content.definition: Cooperative hemoglobin binding modulated by pH, temperature, and 2,3-BPG shifting the dissociation curve.
content.summary: Right shift enhances tissue unloading in acidosis and exercise. Carbon monoxide shifts curve left and reduces capacity.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pain and Nociception
content.definition: Nociceptor activation, spinal gate control, and central sensitization producing acute and chronic pain.
content.summary: Referred pain follows convergent spinal pathways. Opioids modulate pain at spinal and supraspinal sites.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pelvic and Reproductive Anatomy
content.definition: Pelvic viscera, perineum, and male-female reproductive organs with ligamentous support.
content.summary: Pelvic anatomy explains obstetric injury patterns and hernia types. Urogenital diaphragm supports continence mechanisms.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Peptic Ulcer and IBD Pathology
content.definition: Mucosal erosion from acid-pepsin injury versus chronic transmural inflammation in Crohn and ulcerative colitis.
content.summary: H. pylori predisposes to gastric and duodenal ulcers. Crohn skip lesions contrast continuous colitis in UC.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pharmacogenomics
content.definition: Genetic variants altering drug metabolism, receptor response, and adverse effect susceptibility.
content.summary: G6PD deficiency causes hemolysis with oxidant drugs. TPMT variants affect thiopurine toxicity.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Drug Absorption and Distribution
content.definition: Routes of administration, bioavailability, and tissue partitioning governed by physicochemical properties.
content.summary: First-pass metabolism lowers oral bioavailability. Lipophilicity and protein binding affect distribution.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Pneumonia and Lung Abscess Pathology
content.definition: Consolidation patterns from infectious agents with lobar, bronchopneumonic, and interstitial variants.
content.summary: Aspiration pneumonia involves dependent segments. Abscesses form cavitary necrotic collections.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Population Genetics and Hardy-Weinberg
content.definition: Allele frequencies in ideal populations and factors disturbing equilibrium including selection and drift.
content.summary: Hardy-Weinberg assumes random mating and large population. Carrier frequency informs screening programs.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Receptor Pharmacodynamics
content.definition: Agonist and antagonist binding producing graded and quantal dose-response relationships.
content.summary: Efficacy versus potency distinguish full and partial agonists. Competitive antagonism shifts dose-response right.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Renal and Urinary Tract Anatomy
content.definition: Kidneys, ureters, bladder, and urethra with segmental innervation and retroperitoneal relationships.
content.summary: Renal segmental anatomy informs partial nephrectomy and stone management. Ureteric course explains referred pain.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Renal Drug Elimination
content.definition: Glomerular filtration, tubular secretion, and reabsorption determining renal clearance.
content.summary: Dose adjustment required in CKD for renally cleared drugs. Probenecid blocks tubular secretion.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Renal Filtration and GFR
content.definition: Glomerular ultrafiltration driven by Starling forces, with tubular reabsorption and secretion adjusting urine composition to maintain fluid and electrolyte homeostasis.
content.summary: GFR is the clinical index of kidney function. Nephron segment physiology explains diuretic mechanisms and AKI/CKD staging.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Merged kidney_filtration_and_reabsorption (biology) with renal_filtration_and_gfr (medicine_preclinical).

framing.relevance: Core nephron physiology for Step 1 and clinical medicine.
General osmoregulation and nitrogenous waste elimination across taxa.

content.title: Respiratory Embryology
content.definition: Lung bud branching, alveolarization, and surfactant-producing cell differentiation.
content.summary: Alveolar development continues postnatally. Surfactant deficiency causes neonatal respiratory distress syndrome.

framing.relevance: Core embryology & development concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Respiratory Mechanics and Compliance
content.definition: Lung and chest wall elastic recoil, airway resistance, and work of breathing.
content.summary: Compliance changes in ARDS and emphysema alter ventilation. Airway resistance rises in bronchospasm.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Respiratory Tract Anatomy
content.definition: Conducting and respiratory zones from nose to alveoli including pleura and mediastinal relations.
content.summary: Segmental bronchial anatomy guides localization of infiltrates. Pleural spaces explain pneumothorax and effusion mechanics.

framing.relevance: Core gross anatomy concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Screening Test Characteristics
content.definition: Sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, and likelihood ratios evaluating diagnostic tests.
content.summary: High sensitivity rules out disease (SNOUT). High specificity rules in disease (SPIN).

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.
Sensitivity, specificity, and predictive values apply to orthopaedic screening tools and diagnostic tests.

content.title: Sensory Systems Overview
content.definition: Special and somatic senses transducing stimuli via receptor potentials and labeled lines.
content.summary: Receptive fields determine spatial resolution. Lateral inhibition enhances contrast.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Sleep and Consciousness
content.definition: Wake-sleep architecture, reticular activating system, and EEG correlates of arousal states.
content.summary: REM sleep features vivid dreams and paralysis. Coma reflects diffuse arousal system dysfunction.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Spinal Cord Tracts
content.definition: Ascending sensory and descending motor pathways with somatotopic organization.
content.summary: Lateral corticospinal tract mediates voluntary motor control. Dorsal columns carry proprioception.

framing.relevance: Core neuroscience concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Staphylococcus and Streptococcus
content.definition: Catalase-positive staphylococci versus catalase-negative streptococci with toxin and invasive syndromes.
content.summary: S. aureus causes skin abscess and endocarditis. Streptococcal pharyngitis risks rheumatic fever.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Steady-State Drug Concentration
content.definition: Plateau plasma level reached when dosing rate equals elimination rate, approximated after five half-lives.
content.summary: Steady state depends on dose, interval, and half-life. Loading doses accelerate therapeutic levels.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Clinical Study Designs
content.definition: Cross-sectional, case-control, cohort, and randomized controlled trial designs with bias considerations.
content.summary: RCTs minimize confounding via randomization. Case-control studies are efficient for rare outcomes.

framing.relevance: Core behavioral science & epidemiology concept for medical sciences.
RCTs and observational studies underpin orthopaedic evidence including fixation trials and arthroplasty registries.

content.title: Systemic and Pulmonary Circulation
content.definition: Pressure-flow relationships, vascular resistance, and compliance in parallel and series circulations.
content.summary: Pulmonary versus systemic resistance explains shunt physiology. Vascular compliance buffers pulsatile flow.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: TCA Cycle and Electron Transport
content.definition: Mitochondrial oxidation of acetyl-CoA with NADH and FADH2 driving oxidative phosphorylation.
content.summary: ETC inhibitors and uncouplers reduce ATP yield. Inherited ETC defects cause lactic acidosis.

framing.relevance: Core biochemistry & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Thyroid Hormone Synthesis and Action
content.definition: Iodine trapping, organification, coupling, and nuclear receptor-mediated metabolic effects of T3 and T4.
content.summary: TSH drives thyroid growth and hormone release. T3 potentiates beta-adrenergic sensitivity.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Toxicology and Antidotes
content.definition: Recognition of common poisonings and specific antidotes or supportive therapies reversing toxicity.
content.summary: N-acetylcysteine treats acetaminophen overdose. Opioid reversal uses naloxone.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Transplant Immunology and Rejection
content.definition: Hyperacute, acute, and chronic rejection mediated by preformed antibodies and T cell responses.
content.summary: Hyperacute rejection is immediate and antibody mediated. Immunosuppression prevents acute cellular rejection.

framing.relevance: Core immunology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Trinucleotide Repeat Disorders
content.definition: Unstable expansions causing anticipation in Huntington disease, fragile X, and myotonic dystrophy.
content.summary: Anticipation is earlier onset in successive generations. Huntington CAG repeats expand in paternal transmission.

framing.relevance: Core genetics & molecular medicine concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Tubular Reabsorption and Secretion
content.definition: Segment-specific transport reclaiming water, electrolytes, and nutrients while secreting acids and organic solutes.
content.summary: Proximal tubule reabsorbs bulk filtrate. Loop diuretics act on ascending limb Na-K-2Cl cotransport.

framing.relevance: Core physiology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Tumor Staging and Grading
content.definition: Anatomic extent (TNM) and histologic grade predicting prognosis and guiding therapy.
content.summary: Stage reflects spread; grade reflects differentiation. Lymph node involvement upstages most solid tumors.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Urogenital Embryology
content.definition: Intermediate mesoderm forming kidneys, gonads, and internal-external genitalia with duct fusion events.
content.summary: Müllerian and Wolffian duct fate depends on hormones. Renal ascent explains ectopic kidney locations.

framing.relevance: Core embryology & development concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Viral Replication Cycles
content.definition: Attachment, entry, genome replication, assembly, and release with lytic versus lysogenic strategies.
content.summary: Retroviruses integrate proviral DNA. Latent herpesviruses reactivate under immunosuppression.

framing.relevance: Core microbiology & infectious disease concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Vitamin Cofactors and Deficiencies
content.definition: Water- and fat-soluble vitamins serving as coenzymes or antioxidants with deficiency syndromes.
content.summary: Thiamine deficiency causes Wernicke encephalopathy. Vitamin D regulates calcium and phosphate homeostasis.

framing.relevance: Core biochemistry & metabolism concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Volume of Distribution
content.definition: Apparent compartment volume relating total body drug amount to plasma concentration after distribution.
content.summary: Large Vd suggests extensive tissue binding. Vd informs loading dose calculations.

framing.relevance: Core pharmacology concept for medical sciences.

content.title: Wound Healing and Repair
content.definition: Hemostasis, inflammation, proliferation, and remodeling phases restoring tissue integrity.
content.summary: Primary intention minimizes scar. Keloids reflect excessive collagen deposition in susceptible individuals.

framing.relevance: Core pathology concept for medical sciences.
Wound healing biology underpins fracture healing, soft-tissue coverage, and surgical incision management in orthopaedics.

content.title: Atomic Structure and Spectra
content.definition: Electrons in atoms occupy quantized states labeled by quantum numbers, producing characteristic emission and absorption line spectra.
content.summary: Atomic structure explains the periodic table's chemical periodicity at a physical level. Selection rules and fine structure connect theory to precision spectroscopy.

framing.relevance: Foundational modern physics & quantum mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Capacitance and Dielectrics
content.definition: Capacitance quantifies charge stored per volt across conductors; dielectric materials alter capacitance by polarizing in external fields.
content.summary: Capacitors store energy in electric fields and shape transient behavior in circuits. Dielectrics increase capacitance and withstand breakdown limits in devices.

framing.relevance: Foundational electricity & magnetism concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Conservation of Mechanical Energy
content.definition: When only conservative forces perform work, the sum of kinetic and potential energy remains constant for an isolated system.
content.summary: Energy conservation reduces complex force problems to bookkeeping among energy forms. It is especially powerful for motion in gravitational and elastic potentials.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Damped and Driven Oscillations
content.definition: Oscillatory systems subject to dissipative forces or periodic driving, exhibiting decaying amplitude, resonance, and phase lag between drive and response.
content.summary: Real oscillators lose energy to friction or gain energy from drivers. Resonance explains dramatic amplitude growth at matching drive frequencies in mechanical and electrical analogs.

framing.relevance: Foundational waves & oscillations concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Diffraction
content.definition: Bending and spreading of waves around obstacles and through apertures, with intensity patterns governed by wavelength and opening size.
content.summary: Diffraction sets the ultimate resolution limit of optical systems. Single-slit and grating patterns quantify how wave nature dominates when aperture sizes approach wavelength.

framing.relevance: Foundational optics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Direct-Current Circuits
content.definition: Steady current flow through resistors and sources governed by Ohm's law and conservation rules for charge and energy at junctions and loops.
content.summary: DC circuit analysis combines resistors in series and parallel with Kirchhoff's rules. It is the practical foundation for electronics and bioelectric measurements.

framing.relevance: Foundational electricity & magnetism concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Electric Charge and Coulomb's Law
content.definition: Electric charge is a conserved property of matter; stationary point charges exert forces proportional to charge product and inversely proportional to distance squared.
content.summary: Coulomb's law is the electrostatic analog of gravitation with signed charges. It underpins electric fields, potential, and all circuit behavior.

framing.relevance: Foundational electricity & magnetism concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Electric Fields and Potential
content.definition: Electric field is force per unit charge; electric potential is potential energy per unit charge, with field related to the gradient of potential.
content.summary: Fields and potentials provide spatial maps of electrostatic influence. Potential differences drive current in conductors and store energy in capacitors.

framing.relevance: Foundational electricity & magnetism concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Electromagnetic Induction
content.definition: Changing magnetic flux through a loop induces an electromotive force, described by Faraday's law and Lenz's law for polarity.
content.summary: Induction converts between mechanical and electrical energy in generators and transformers. It completes the dynamic link between electric and magnetic phenomena.

framing.relevance: Foundational electricity & magnetism concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Electromagnetic Spectrum and Light
content.definition: Light comprises transverse electromagnetic waves spanning wavelengths from radio to gamma rays, each interacting with matter through absorption, emission, and scattering.
content.summary: The spectrum organizes radiation by frequency and energy. Understanding wave–particle duality begins with classifying how different wavelengths probe structure and dynamics.

framing.relevance: Foundational optics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Entropy and the Second Law
content.definition: Entropy measures dispersal of energy; in isolated systems it never decreases, and spontaneous processes increase total entropy.
content.summary: The second law explains irreversibility and the direction of spontaneous change. Entropy connects macroscopic heat flow to microscopic disorder and statistical mechanics.

framing.relevance: Foundational thermodynamics & statistical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: First Law of Thermodynamics
content.definition: Energy conservation for thermodynamic systems: change in internal energy equals heat added minus work done by the system.
content.summary: The first law generalizes mechanical energy conservation to include thermal energy. It governs processes in engines, refrigerators, and chemical reactions at constant volume or pressure.

framing.relevance: Foundational thermodynamics & statistical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Fluid Flow and Bernoulli's Equation
content.definition: Conservation of energy along streamlines relates pressure, speed, and height for ideal, incompressible, steady flow.
content.summary: Bernoulli's equation connects flow speed to pressure in pipes, wings, and venturis. It is the starting model for lift, blood flow, and plumbing design.

framing.relevance: Foundational fluid mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Geometric Optics
content.definition: Ray approximation for light propagation through reflection and refraction at interfaces, including mirrors, lenses, and image formation.
content.summary: Geometric optics uses straight-line rays where wavelengths are small compared to features. Lens and mirror equations predict real and virtual images in instruments.

framing.relevance: Foundational optics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Heat Engines and the Carnot Cycle
content.definition: Devices that convert thermal energy to work through cyclic processes; the Carnot cycle sets the maximum efficiency limit between two reservoirs.
content.summary: Heat engines illustrate the practical consequences of thermodynamic laws. Carnot efficiency depends only on reservoir temperatures and motivates the definition of entropy.

framing.title_in_context: Heat Engines and the Carnot Cycle
framing.relevance: Foundational thermodynamics & statistical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

hierarchy_location.topic: Heat Engines and the Carnot Cycle

content.title: Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
content.definition: Conjugate pairs such as position and momentum cannot both be known with arbitrary precision; their uncertainties satisfy a lower bound proportional to Planck's constant.
content.summary: Uncertainty is a fundamental limit on simultaneous measurement, not merely instrumental error. It constrains atomic size, zero-point energy, and microscopy resolution.

framing.relevance: Foundational modern physics & quantum mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Hydrostatic Pressure and Buoyancy
content.definition: Pressure in a fluid increases with depth; buoyant force on a submerged body equals the weight of displaced fluid.
content.summary: Fluid statics explains pressure variation in atmospheres and oceans. Archimedes' principle accounts for floating, sinking, and apparent weight loss in fluids.

framing.relevance: Foundational fluid mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Ideal Gas Law
content.definition: Equation of state PV = nRT relating pressure, volume, amount, and temperature for gases whose molecular interactions are negligible.
content.summary: The ideal gas law connects macroscopic variables to microscopic behavior through the gas constant. It is the starting point for kinetic theory and engine cycles.

framing.relevance: Foundational thermodynamics & statistical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: One-Dimensional Kinematics
content.definition: Description of motion along a straight line using displacement, velocity, and acceleration as functions of time, without reference to the forces that cause the motion.
content.summary: One-dimensional kinematics provides the vocabulary and equations for how position changes over time. Constant-acceleration relations link initial and final states and underpin nearly all introductory mechanics.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Linear Momentum and Collisions
content.definition: Linear momentum is mass times velocity; its rate of change equals net external force. In isolated systems, total momentum is conserved through elastic and inelastic collisions.
content.summary: Momentum conservation explains collision outcomes when forces are impulsive or unknown. Impulse–momentum relations complement energy methods for impact problems.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Magnetic Fields and Forces
content.definition: Moving charges and currents experience forces perpendicular to both velocity and magnetic field; fields are produced by currents and permanent magnets.
content.summary: Magnetic forces do no work on charges but deflect trajectories and torques on current loops. They explain motors, mass spectrometers, and the basis of induction.

framing.relevance: Foundational electricity & magnetism concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Matter Waves and de Broglie Wavelength
content.definition: Every particle has an associated wavelength inversely proportional to momentum, unifying wave and particle descriptions of matter.
content.summary: de Broglie waves explain electron diffraction and discrete atomic orbits. They extend wave mechanics from light to all massive particles.

framing.title_in_context: Matter Waves and de Broglie Wavelength
framing.relevance: Foundational modern physics & quantum mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

hierarchy_location.topic: Matter Waves and de Broglie Wavelength

content.title: Maxwell's Equations and Electromagnetic Waves
content.definition: Unified laws of electricity and magnetism predicting self-propagating transverse waves with speed determined by permeability and permittivity of the medium.
content.summary: Maxwell's synthesis shows light is an electromagnetic wave. The equations underpin optics, wireless communication, and relativistic invariance of field laws.

framing.relevance: Foundational electricity & magnetism concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Mechanical Waves
content.definition: Disturbances that propagate through a medium via coupled oscillations of neighboring elements, characterized by wavelength, frequency, speed, and amplitude.
content.summary: Mechanical waves transport energy without net transport of matter. The wave equation links speed to medium properties and underlies sound, seismic waves, and string vibrations.

framing.relevance: Foundational waves & oscillations concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Newton's Laws of Motion
content.definition: Three principles relating forces to changes in motion: inertia, proportionality of net force to acceleration, and equal-and-opposite interaction pairs.
content.summary: Newton's laws translate force diagrams into predictive equations of motion. They are the central organizing framework for classical mechanics from everyday objects to celestial orbits.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Nuclear Decay Modes
content.definition: Unstable nuclei emit alpha particles, beta particles, or gamma rays, changing atomic number and mass number according to conservation laws.
content.summary: Each decay mode has distinct penetrating power and biological hazard. Decay schemes and balancing rules predict daughter nuclei and radiation spectra.

framing.relevance: Foundational nuclear physics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Nuclear Fission and Fusion
content.definition: Fission splits heavy nuclei into lighter fragments; fusion combines light nuclei into heavier ones, both releasing energy when products have higher binding energy per nucleon.
content.summary: Controlled fission powers reactors; uncontrolled fission yields weapons. Fusion drives stars and promises clean energy if confinement challenges are met.

framing.relevance: Foundational nuclear physics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Nuclear Structure and Binding Energy
content.definition: Atomic nuclei consist of protons and neutrons bound by the strong force; mass defect relative to constituent nucleons equals nuclear binding energy.
content.summary: Binding energy per nucleon peaks near iron, explaining energy release in fusion and fission. Nuclear models account for stability and magic numbers.

framing.relevance: Foundational nuclear physics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Photoelectric and Compton Effects
content.definition: Experimental evidence that light transfers energy in discrete quanta and that photons carry momentum, establishing particle aspects of electromagnetic radiation.
content.summary: These effects motivated quantum theory by showing energy and momentum quantization of light. Work-function and wavelength-shift measurements link photon energy to frequency.

framing.relevance: Foundational modern physics & quantum mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Polarization
content.definition: Orientation of the transverse electric field oscillation in electromagnetic waves, altered by polarizers, reflection, and birefringent materials.
content.summary: Polarization reveals the vector character of light. It is exploited in LCD displays, stress analysis, and filtering scattered glare.

framing.relevance: Foundational optics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Projectile Motion
content.definition: Two-dimensional motion of an object under uniform gravitational acceleration, decomposed into independent horizontal and vertical components.
content.summary: Projectile motion applies vector decomposition to predict trajectories, range, and time of flight. It bridges one-dimensional kinematics and Newton's laws in two dimensions.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Radiation Detection and Dosimetry
content.definition: Measurement of ionizing radiation intensity and absorbed dose using detectors such as Geiger counters, scintillators, and dosimeters calibrated in sieverts or grays.
content.summary: Detection methods map particle type and energy to observable signals. Dosimetry quantifies biological risk and guides shielding in medicine and industry.

framing.relevance: Foundational nuclear physics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Rotational Dynamics
content.definition: Extension of Newton's laws to rigid bodies rotating about a fixed axis, using torque, moment of inertia, and angular acceleration.
content.summary: Rotational dynamics parallels translational mechanics with angular analogs of force, mass, and acceleration. It explains rolling, pulleys, and gyroscopic behavior in macroscopic systems.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Schrödinger Equation and Bound States
content.definition: Wave function evolution governed by a linear differential equation whose normalizable solutions in potentials yield quantized energy levels.
content.summary: The Schrödinger equation replaces classical trajectories with probability amplitudes. Particle-in-a-box and hydrogen-like models predict discrete spectra.

framing.relevance: Foundational modern physics & quantum mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Simple Harmonic Motion
content.definition: Periodic motion in which restoring force is proportional to displacement from equilibrium, producing sinusoidal position, velocity, and acceleration.
content.summary: Simple harmonic motion models springs, small pendulums, and molecular vibrations. Its solutions introduce angular frequency, phase, and the role of initial conditions.

framing.relevance: Foundational waves & oscillations concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Sound Waves and the Doppler Effect
content.definition: Longitudinal pressure waves in elastic media, including frequency shifts observed when source and observer move relative to one another.
content.summary: Sound applies wave mechanics to compressible fluids. The Doppler effect provides a measurable link between relative motion and observed frequency in acoustics and electromagnetics.

framing.title_in_context: Sound Waves and the Doppler Effect
framing.relevance: Foundational waves & oscillations concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

hierarchy_location.topic: Sound Waves and the Doppler Effect

content.title: Special Relativity
content.definition: Framework in which the laws of physics are invariant in all inertial frames, implying time dilation, length contraction, and equivalence of mass and energy.
content.summary: Special relativity revises kinematics at speeds approaching light. E = mc² and Lorentz transformations unify space and time in high-energy physics.

framing.relevance: Foundational modern physics & quantum mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Standing Waves and Resonance
content.definition: Confined waves that form stationary nodes and antinodes at discrete resonant frequencies determined by boundary conditions.
content.summary: Standing waves explain musical instrument harmonics and cavity modes. Quantized resonant frequencies foreshadow bound states in quantum mechanics.

framing.relevance: Foundational waves & oscillations concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Temperature and Heat
content.definition: Temperature quantifies thermal equilibrium and average microscopic kinetic energy; heat is energy transferred between systems because of a temperature difference.
content.summary: Distinguishing temperature from heat is essential for thermodynamic reasoning. Calorimetry links measurable temperature changes to energy exchange.

framing.relevance: Foundational thermodynamics & statistical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Universal Gravitation
content.definition: Every mass attracts every other mass with a force proportional to the product of masses and inversely proportional to the square of their separation.
content.summary: Newton's law of gravitation unifies terrestrial falling with planetary orbits. Combined with energy and momentum methods, it predicts satellite motion and orbital stability.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Viscosity and Laminar Flow
content.definition: Viscous fluids resist shear flow; laminar pipe flow has parabolic velocity profiles with flow rate proportional to pressure gradient and fourth power of radius.
content.summary: Viscosity captures internal friction absent in ideal fluids. Poiseuille's law quantifies resistance in biological vessels and microfluidic devices.

framing.relevance: Foundational fluid mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Wave Interference in Optics
content.definition: Superposition of coherent light waves producing intensity patterns from path-length differences, as in double-slit and thin-film interference.
content.summary: Optical interference demonstrates the wave nature of light with measurable fringe patterns. It enables precision metrology and spectroscopic resolution.

framing.relevance: Foundational optics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Wave Superposition and Interference
content.definition: When waves overlap, displacements add algebraically, producing constructive and destructive interference patterns depending on phase difference.
content.summary: Superposition is the linearity principle for waves. Interference patterns reveal path-length differences and are central to acoustics, optics, and quantum probability amplitudes.

framing.relevance: Foundational waves & oscillations concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Work and Kinetic Energy
content.definition: Work is the line integral of force along displacement; the work–energy theorem states that net work equals the change in kinetic energy of a particle or rigid system.
content.summary: The work–energy theorem offers an alternative to force-based analysis for problems involving speeds and distances. It connects force, displacement, and energy transfer in a single scalar relation.

framing.relevance: Foundational classical mechanics concept for modeling physical systems quantitatively.

content.title: Adolescent Development
content.definition: Physical puberty, identity formation, peer influence, and brain maturation during the transition from childhood to adulthood.
content.summary: Adolescence involves search for autonomy and identity. Prefrontal maturation lags limbic development.

framing.relevance: Core developmental psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Aggression and Prosocial Behavior
content.definition: Harmful behavior toward others contrasted with helping, cooperation, and altruism shaped by biological, learning, and situational factors.
content.summary: Frustration–aggression and social learning explain violence. Bystander intervention depends on diffusion of responsibility.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Aging and Cognitive Change
content.definition: Normative and pathological changes in cognition, social roles, and health in late adulthood including dementia risk factors.
content.summary: Crystallized knowledge often persists while processing speed declines. Cognitive reserve moderates decline.

framing.relevance: Core developmental psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Anxiety Disorders
content.definition: Excessive fear and avoidance including generalized anxiety, panic, phobic, and related disorders disrupting daily functioning.
content.summary: Anxiety disorders involve hyperarousal and catastrophic cognitions. Exposure-based therapies target avoidance.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Psychopathology classification owned by psychology_neuroscience; medicine_clinical adds management framing (merged duplicate clinical L3).

framing.relevance: DSM-based psychopathology classification and behavioral mechanisms.
Clinical management with CBT, SSRIs, and short-term anxiolytics.

content.title: Attachment Theory
content.definition: Enduring emotional bonds between infants and caregivers shaping security, exploration, and later relational patterns.
content.summary: Strange Situation classifications predict social behavior. Internal working models guide expectations in relationships.

framing.relevance: Core developmental psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Attention and Consciousness
content.definition: Selective allocation of limited processing resources to stimuli and tasks, shaping awareness and performance on concurrent activities.
content.summary: Attention filters sensory input and working memory load. Failures of attention produce inattentional blindness.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Auditory Perception
content.definition: Transduction of sound waves into neural codes for pitch, loudness, and localization along the auditory pathway.
content.summary: Place and temporal codes explain pitch perception. Cochlear mechanics map frequency to basilar location.

framing.relevance: Core sensation & perception concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Big Five Model
content.definition: Five broad trait factors—openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, and neuroticism—describing major personality variance.
content.summary: The Big Five show cross-cultural replicability. Traits predict occupational and health outcomes modestly.

framing.relevance: Core personality & individual differences concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Biological Constraints on Learning
content.definition: Evolutionary predispositions limiting what associations are easily learned, including taste aversion and preparedness.
content.summary: Species-specific biases explain rapid taste–illness learning. Not all behaviors are equally conditionable.

framing.relevance: Core learning & conditioning concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Brain Anatomy Overview
content.definition: Major brain regions—brainstem, cerebellum, limbic system, and cerebral cortex—and their roles in vital functions, emotion, and cognition.
content.summary: Forebrain structures support higher cognition. Subcortical nuclei regulate arousal, motivation, and motor programs.

framing.relevance: Core biological psychology & neuroscience concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Cerebral Lateralization
content.definition: Functional specialization of left and right hemispheres for language, spatial processing, and other cognitive domains.
content.summary: Split-brain studies reveal hemispheric contributions. Lateralization is probabilistic rather than absolute.

framing.relevance: Core biological psychology & neuroscience concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Classical Conditioning
content.definition: Learning in which a neutral conditioned stimulus acquires the ability to elicit a response after pairing with an unconditioned stimulus.
content.summary: Pavlovian conditioning explains reflexive associations. Extinction and spontaneous recovery demonstrate flexibility.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Medicine preclinical behavioral science (patient adherence, aversive conditioning) is a domain context — not a separate L3.

framing.relevance: Foundation of associative learning in behavioral psychology.
Behavioral medicine and patient adherence — conditioned responses to clinical cues.

content.title: Cognitive Biases
content.definition: Systematic deviations from normative reasoning such as confirmation bias, anchoring, and representativeness affecting beliefs and decisions.
content.summary: Biases arise from efficient heuristics applied inappropriately. Debiasing strategies slow deliberative processing.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Cognitive Maps and Latent Learning
content.definition: Learning that occurs without immediate reinforcement and is revealed later, including mental representations of spatial layouts.
content.summary: Tolman's maze studies support cognitive maps. Expectancies guide goal-directed behavior beyond stimulus–response links.

framing.relevance: Core learning & conditioning concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Conformity and Obedience
content.definition: Adjustment of behavior or beliefs to align with group norms or authority commands, as demonstrated in classic social psychology experiments.
content.summary: Normative and informational influence drive conformity. Situational factors powerfully shape compliance.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Correlation and Regression
content.definition: Quantifying linear association between variables and predicting outcomes from predictors while distinguishing correlation from causation.
content.summary: Pearson r measures linear association strength. Regression models prediction with residual analysis.

framing.relevance: Core research methods & statistics concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Cultural Influences on Behavior
content.definition: How shared values, norms, and practices across cultures shape cognition, emotion, self-concept, and social behavior.
content.summary: Individualist and collectivist cultures differ in self-construal. Cultural competence requires contextual understanding.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Decision Making and Judgment
content.definition: Evaluation of options under uncertainty using normative models and descriptive heuristics that can produce systematic biases.
content.summary: Expected utility contrasts with bounded rationality. Framing and availability shape risky choices.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Diagnostic Classification and DSM
content.definition: Multiaxial descriptive system categorizing mental disorders by symptom clusters, duration, and functional impairment for clinical communication.
content.summary: DSM criteria standardize diagnosis. Comorbidity and dimensional models challenge categorical boundaries.

framing.relevance: Core abnormal psychology & psychopathology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Eating Disorders
content.definition: Disturbances in eating behavior and body image including anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, and binge-eating disorder.
content.summary: Sociocultural and biological factors interact in eating disorders. Medical complications require multidisciplinary care.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Psychopathology classification owned by psychology_neuroscience; medicine_clinical adds management framing (merged duplicate clinical L3).

framing.relevance: DSM-based psychopathology classification and behavioral mechanisms.
Medical stabilization and psychotherapy in inpatient and outpatient settings.

content.title: Endocrine Stress Response
content.definition: Activation of the hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal axis releasing cortisol to mobilize energy and modulate stress-related behavior and cognition.
content.summary: Acute stress enhances vigilance; chronic stress can impair health. Feedback terminates HPA activation.

framing.relevance: Core biological psychology & neuroscience concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Erikson's Psychosocial Stages
content.definition: Eight psychosocial crises across the lifespan each requiring resolution of a central conflict for healthy personality development.
content.summary: Trust, identity, and generativity mark key crises. Unresolved stages may re-emerge under stress.

framing.relevance: Core developmental psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Ethics in Psychological Research
content.definition: Principles of informed consent, confidentiality, debriefing, and minimization of harm governing human and animal research.
content.summary: IRB review balances scientific value with participant welfare. Vulnerable populations require added safeguards.

framing.relevance: Core research methods & statistics concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Executive Function
content.definition: Higher-order processes including working memory updating, inhibitory control, and cognitive flexibility coordinating goal-directed behavior.
content.summary: Prefrontal cortex supports executive function. Deficits appear in ADHD and frontal lobe injury.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Experimental Design Basics
content.definition: Manipulation of independent variables with random assignment and control of confounds to infer causal relationships.
content.summary: True experiments isolate causation. Control groups and blinding reduce bias.

framing.relevance: Core research methods & statistics concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Extinction and Spontaneous Recovery
content.definition: Weakening of conditioned responding when the conditioned stimulus is presented without the unconditioned stimulus, with possible return after delay.
content.summary: Extinction is new learning inhibiting prior associations. Context change can renew responding.

framing.relevance: Core learning & conditioning concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Group Dynamics
content.definition: Patterns of interaction within groups including roles, cohesion, social facilitation, and groupthink affecting collective performance.
content.summary: Cohesion can help or hinder decision quality. Social loafing reduces individual effort in group tasks.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Gustation and Olfaction
content.definition: Chemoreception of taste and smell molecules binding receptors to produce flavor perception and environmental chemical detection.
content.summary: Taste qualities combine with retronasal smell for flavor. Olfaction links closely to memory and emotion.

framing.relevance: Core sensation & perception concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Humanistic Theory
content.definition: Emphasis on self-actualization, personal growth, and inherent human potential within a phenomenological view of experience.
content.summary: Rogers' unconditional positive regard supports growth. Maslow's hierarchy prioritizes motivational needs.

framing.relevance: Core personality & individual differences concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Theories of Intelligence
content.definition: Frameworks describing general and specialized cognitive abilities, their measurement, and environmental and genetic contributions.
content.summary: Psychometric models posit g and specific factors. Multiple intelligences and emotional intelligence broaden the construct.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Interpersonal Attraction
content.definition: Factors such as proximity, similarity, reciprocity, and physical attractiveness influencing liking and romantic partnership formation.
content.summary: Reward models explain attraction dynamics. Attachment styles carry into adult relationships.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Language and Cognition
content.definition: Mental representations and rules underlying comprehension and production of spoken, signed, and written language.
content.summary: Language localizes partly in left hemisphere regions. Development and injury reveal dissociations among linguistic components.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Leadership and Social Influence
content.definition: Processes by which individuals guide, motivate, and persuade groups toward goals through transactional or transformational styles.
content.summary: Effective leadership balances task and relational behaviors. Charisma and legitimacy affect follower commitment.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Memory Encoding, Storage, and Retrieval
content.definition: Acquisition, maintenance, and recovery of information across sensory, short-term, and long-term memory systems with reconstructive processes.
content.summary: Encoding strategies and retrieval cues affect recall. Forgetting reflects decay, interference, and retrieval failure.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Metacognition
content.definition: Monitoring and regulating one's own cognitive processes, including knowledge of learning strategies and confidence in judgments.
content.summary: Metacognitive accuracy improves self-regulated learning. Overconfidence is a common calibration error.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Mood Disorders
content.definition: Disturbances of emotional tone including major depressive and bipolar disorders with episodic changes in mood, energy, and cognition.
content.summary: Biopsychosocial models integrate genetic, cognitive, and environmental risk. Recurrence is common without treatment.

framing.relevance: Core abnormal psychology & psychopathology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Moral Development
content.definition: Progression in reasoning about justice, care, and social rules from preconventional through postconventional levels.
content.summary: Kohlberg's stages emphasize justice reasoning. Social domain theory distinguishes moral and conventional norms.

framing.relevance: Core developmental psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Multisensory Integration
content.definition: Combination of inputs from multiple sensory modalities into coherent percepts and improved detection when cues coincide.
content.summary: Cross-modal binding supports spatial orientation. Ventriloquism effect illustrates visual dominance.

framing.relevance: Core sensation & perception concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Nervous System Organization
content.definition: Division of the nervous system into central and peripheral components with functional subsystems for sensory input, integration, and motor output.
content.summary: Structural organization maps to functional roles. Spinal reflex arcs operate below conscious control.

framing.relevance: Core biological psychology & neuroscience concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Neural Conduction and Myelin
content.definition: Propagation of electrical signals along axons enhanced by myelination and saltatory conduction increasing conduction velocity.
content.summary: Myelin insulates axons and reduces capacitance. Demyelination impairs timing of neural communication.

framing.relevance: Core biological psychology & neuroscience concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Neuroplasticity
content.definition: Experience-dependent changes in synaptic strength, connectivity, and sometimes neurogenesis underlying learning and recovery from injury.
content.summary: Plasticity operates on multiple timescales from long-term potentiation to cortical remapping. Critical periods constrain development.

framing.relevance: Core biological psychology & neuroscience concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Observational Learning
content.definition: Acquisition of behaviors by watching models, involving attention, retention, reproduction, and motivation per social learning theory.
content.summary: Bandura's Bobo doll studies show imitation of modeled aggression. Vicarious reinforcement affects performance.

framing.relevance: Core learning & conditioning concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Operant Conditioning
content.definition: Learning through consequences in which reinforced behaviors increase and punished behaviors decrease in frequency.
content.summary: Skinner's operant paradigm shapes voluntary behavior. Reinforcement schedules affect resistance to extinction.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Medicine preclinical behavioral science (reinforcement in adherence) is a domain context — not a separate L3.

framing.relevance: Voluntary behavior shaped by reinforcement and punishment.
Reinforcement schedules in patient behavior change and adherence programs.

content.title: Perceptual Organization and Gestalt Principles
content.definition: Grouping of sensory elements into unified percepts using principles such as proximity, similarity, closure, and figure–ground segregation.
content.summary: Gestalt laws predict how wholes emerge from parts. Top-down context constrains interpretation.

framing.relevance: Core sensation & perception concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Personality Disorders Overview
content.definition: Enduring maladaptive patterns of cognition, affect, and interpersonal functioning deviating from cultural expectations.
content.summary: Clusters group odd-eccentric, dramatic, and anxious-fearful patterns. Traits exist on continua with normality.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Psychopathology classification owned by psychology_neuroscience; medicine_clinical adds management framing (merged duplicate clinical L3).

framing.relevance: DSM-based psychopathology classification and behavioral mechanisms.
Cluster-based clinical approach including DBT for borderline personality disorder.

content.title: Piaget's Cognitive Stages
content.definition: Sequential stages of cognitive development from sensorimotor through formal operations characterized by qualitatively different reasoning modes.
content.summary: Assimilation and accommodation drive stage transitions. Tasks reveal conservation and egocentrism in childhood.

framing.relevance: Core developmental psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Prejudice and Stereotypes
content.definition: Generalized beliefs and attitudes about social groups that can bias perception, memory, and behavior toward group members.
content.summary: Stereotypes simplify social information at accuracy cost. Contact and superordinate goals reduce prejudice.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Prenatal Development
content.definition: Stages of growth from zygote through embryo and fetus, including teratogenic risks and maturation of organ systems before birth.
content.summary: Critical periods heighten vulnerability to environmental insults. Prenatal nutrition and stress affect outcomes.

framing.relevance: Core developmental psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Problem Solving and Reasoning
content.definition: Goal-directed cognitive operations including algorithms, heuristics, and deductive or inductive inference to reach solutions.
content.summary: Problem representation guides strategy choice. Insight and fixation illustrate non-linear solution paths.

framing.relevance: Core cognitive psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Psychodynamic Perspective
content.definition: Personality shaped by unconscious drives, early experiences, and defense mechanisms mediating conflict between id, ego, and superego.
content.summary: Freudian theory emphasizes unconscious motivation. Modern psychodynamic work focuses on relational patterns.

framing.relevance: Core personality & individual differences concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Psychopharmacology Basics
content.definition: Effects of drugs on neurotransmission and behavior through agonism, antagonism, reuptake inhibition, and enzyme modulation.
content.summary: Drug mechanisms map to clinical indications and side effects. Tolerance and dependence involve receptor adaptation.

framing.relevance: Core biological psychology & neuroscience concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Reinforcement Schedules
content.definition: Patterns of delivering reinforcement—fixed or variable ratio and interval—affecting response rate and persistence.
content.summary: Variable ratio schedules produce high steady responding. Partial reinforcement slows extinction.

framing.relevance: Core learning & conditioning concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Reliability and Validity
content.definition: Consistency of measurement over time and items (reliability) and whether a measure captures its intended construct (validity).
content.summary: Test–retest and internal consistency assess reliability. Construct validity integrates multiple evidence sources.

framing.relevance: Core research methods & statistics concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Sampling and Generalization
content.definition: Selection of participants or cases from a population and limits on extending findings beyond the studied sample.
content.summary: Random sampling supports population inference. Convenience samples constrain external validity.

framing.relevance: Core research methods & statistics concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Schizophrenia Spectrum Disorders
content.definition: Disorders featuring positive symptoms such as hallucinations and delusions, negative symptoms, and cognitive disorganization.
content.summary: Dopamine dysregulation and neurodevelopmental factors contribute. Functional outcome varies with support and treatment.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Psychopathology classification owned by psychology_neuroscience; medicine_clinical adds management framing (merged duplicate clinical L3).

framing.relevance: DSM-based psychopathology classification and behavioral mechanisms.
Antipsychotic pharmacotherapy and psychosocial rehabilitation.

content.title: Self-Concept and Identity
content.definition: Beliefs about oneself and integrated sense of identity across roles, values, and group memberships guiding motivation and behavior.
content.summary: Self-esteem and possible selves influence goals. Identity exploration peaks in adolescence.

framing.relevance: Core personality & individual differences concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Sensory Thresholds and Signal Detection
content.definition: Minimum stimulus intensity for detection and signal detection theory separating sensitivity from response bias in perceptual judgments.
content.summary: Absolute and difference thresholds quantify sensitivity. Signal detection models decision criteria in noisy environments.

framing.relevance: Core sensation & perception concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Social Cognition and Attribution
content.definition: How people perceive, interpret, and explain the behavior of themselves and others using internal and external attributions.
content.summary: Fundamental attribution error favors dispositional causes for others' behavior. Schemas guide rapid social judgments.

framing.relevance: Core social & cultural psychology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Somatosensation and Pain
content.definition: Cutaneous and proprioceptive senses including touch, temperature, pain, and body position mediated by specialized receptors.
content.summary: Pain involves nociceptive and neuropathic components. Gate control theory modulates pain signals centrally.

framing.relevance: Core sensation & perception concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Substance Use Disorders
content.definition: Problematic pattern of substance use leading to clinically significant impairment or distress with tolerance and withdrawal features.
content.summary: Reward pathways underlie addiction. Motivational interviewing and MAT support recovery.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Psychopathology classification owned by psychology_neuroscience; medicine_clinical adds management framing (merged duplicate clinical L3).

framing.relevance: DSM-based psychopathology classification and behavioral mechanisms.
Withdrawal management and maintenance therapies in clinical practice.

content.title: Survey and Observational Methods
content.definition: Non-experimental techniques collecting self-report or naturally occurring behavior data with attention to sampling and bias.
content.summary: Surveys enable large-scale attitude measurement. Observational coding requires operational definitions.

framing.relevance: Core research methods & statistics concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Temperament and Heritability
content.definition: Innate behavioral and emotional reactivity contributing to adult personality with substantial genetic and environmental components.
content.summary: Twin studies estimate heritability of traits. Temperament in infancy foreshadows later styles.

framing.relevance: Core personality & individual differences concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Trait Theories of Personality
content.definition: Stable dimensions of individual differences describing consistent patterns of thought, emotion, and behavior across situations.
content.summary: Trait models quantify personality on continuous scales. Consistency interacts with situational strength.

framing.relevance: Core personality & individual differences concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Trauma and PTSD
content.definition: Persistent re-experiencing, avoidance, negative cognitions, and hyperarousal following exposure to traumatic stressors.
content.summary: PTSD involves fear circuitry dysregulation. Trauma-informed care emphasizes safety and stabilization.

framing.relevance: Core abnormal psychology & psychopathology concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.

content.title: Visual Perception
content.definition: Processing of light patterns from retina through cortical pathways to construct color, form, motion, and depth percepts.
content.summary: Feature detectors and parallel pathways analyze visual scenes. Optical illusions reveal constructive processing.

framing.relevance: Core sensation & perception concept for understanding behavior, cognition, and mental processes.
